{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+е’ҢжіүеҢ—йғЁгҒ®йҰ–й•·еў“зі»иӯңгҖҢе’ҢжіүеҢ—йғЁгҒ®йҰ–й•·зі»иӯңгҖҚгҖҺе’ҢжіүеёӮгҒ®иҖғеҸӨгғ»еҸӨд»Јгғ»дёӯдё– пјҲе’ҢжіүеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ6пјүгҖҸ е’ҢжіүеёӮеҸІз·ЁгҒ•гӮ“е§”е“Ўдјҡз·ЁгҖҒе’ҢжіүеёӮгғ»гҒҺгӮҮгҒҶгҒӣгҒ„гҖҒ2013е№ҙгҖҒpp. 65-70гҖӮ !colspan=2|зҫӨ!!еҸӨеўіеҗҚ!!еҪўзҠ¶!!иҰҸжЁЎ!!зҜүйҖ жҷӮжңҹ |- |еҢ—|| ||[[е’Ңжіүй»„йҮ‘еЎҡеҸӨеўі]]||[[еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі]]||94m||4cжң« |- | ||еҚ—||дёёз¬ еұұеҸӨеўі||еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі||96m||4cжң« |- | ||еҚ—||йҚӢеЎҡеҸӨеўі||[[еҶҶеўі]]||60-70m||5cеҲқ |- | ||еҚ—||зҺӢеЎҡеҸӨеўі||[[еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі]]||46m||5cдёӯ |- |еҢ—|| ||'''дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі'''||еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі||60m||5cеүҚ |- |еҢ—|| ||гӮ«гғӢгғӨеЎҡеҸӨеўі||еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі||дёҚжҳҺ||5cеҫҢ |- |еҢ—|| ||[[еӨ§ең’еҸӨеўі]]||еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі||50m||5cжң« |- |еҢ—|| ||[[еҜҢжңЁи»ҠеЎҡеҸӨеўі]]||еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі||48m||6cдёӯ |- | ||еҚ—||зӢҗеЎҡеҸӨеўі||еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі||58m||6cеҫҢ |}
еӨ§йҳӘеәңеҚ—йғЁгҖҒдҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷөеҢ—иҘҝйә“гҒ®дҪҺдҪҚж®өдёҳдёҠгҒ«зҜүйҖ гҒ•гӮҢгҒҹеҸӨеўігҒ§гҒӮгӮӢгҖӮгҖҢиІқеҗ№еұұгҖҚгҒ®еҸӨеўіеҗҚгҒҜгҖҒ[[жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј]]гҒ®дёҖжҸҶпјҲеҚғеҺҹйЁ’еӢ•пјүгҒ«гҒҠгҒ„гҒҰиҫІж°‘гҒҢжі•иһәиІқгӮ’еҗҲеӣігҒ«еҪ“ең°гҒ«йӣҶгҒҫгҒЈгҒҹгҒ“гҒЁгҒ«з”ұжқҘгҒҷгӮӢеҸІи·ЎиӘ¬жҳҺжқҝгҖӮгҖӮгҒ“гӮҢгҒҫгҒ§гҒ«йғЁеҲҶзҡ„гҒӘзҷәжҺҳиӘҝжҹ»гҒҢе®ҹж–ҪгҒ•гӮҢгҒҰгҒ„гӮӢ{{Sfn|иІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўіпјҲеҸӨеўіпјү|1989}}гҖӮ еўіеҪўгҒҜеүҚж–№йғЁгҒҢзҹӯе°ҸгҒӘеёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўгҒ®еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеҪўгҒ§гҖҒеүҚж–№йғЁгӮ’еҢ—иҘҝж–№гҒ«еҗ‘гҒ‘гӮӢгҖӮеўідёҳй•·гҒҜзҙ„60гғЎгғјгғҲгғ«гҖҒеҫҢеҶҶйғЁгҒҜзӣҙеҫ„50гғЎгғјгғҲгғ«гғ»й«ҳгҒ•7.7гғЎгғјгғҲгғ«гӮ’жё¬гӮӢгҖӮеўідёҳиЎЁйқўгҒ§гҒҜ[[и‘әзҹі]]гғ»[[еҹҙијӘ]]пјҲ[[еҶҶзӯ’еҹҙијӘ]]гғ»[[еҪўиұЎеҹҙијӘ]]пјүгҒҢиӘҚгӮҒгӮүгӮҢгӮӢгҒ»гҒӢгҖҒеўідёҳе‘ЁеӣІгҒ«гҒҜе‘Ёжҝ гҒҢе·ЎгӮүгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢ{{Sfn|иІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўіпјҲеҸӨеўіпјү|1989}}гҖӮдё»дҪ“йғЁгҒ®еҹӢи‘¬ж–ҪиЁӯгҒҜжҳҺгӮүгҒӢгҒ§гҒӘгҒ„гҖӮеҮәеңҹе“ҒгҒ«гҒҜеҹҙијӘгҒ®гҒ»гҒӢж»‘зҹіиЈҪж§ҪгҒҢгҒӮгӮӢ{{Sfn|иІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўіпјҲеҸӨеўіпјү|1989}}гҖӮ гҒ“гҒ®дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўігҒҜгҖҒ[[еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј]]дёӯжңҹгҒ®[[5дё–зҙҖ]]еүҚеҚҠй ғгҒ®зҜүйҖ гҒЁжҺЁе®ҡгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢгҖӮе’Ңжіүең°ж–№еҢ—йғЁгҒ§гҒҜйҰ–й•·еў“еҸӨеўігҒЁгҒ—гҒҰеҢ—зҫӨгғ»еҚ—зҫӨгҒ®2зҫӨгҒҢиӘҚгӮҒгӮүгӮҢгӮӢгҒҢгҖҒеҢ—зҫӨгғ»еҚ—зҫӨгҒ«гҒҜгҒқгӮҢгҒһгӮҢжҷӮжңҹзҡ„з©әзҷҪгҒҢиӘҚгӮҒгӮүгӮҢгӮӢгҒ“гҒЁгҒӢгӮүдёЎзҫӨгҒҢијӘз•ӘеҲ¶гҒ§йҰ–й•·гӮ’жӢ…гҒЈгҒҹгҒЁжҺЁе®ҡгҒ•гӮҢгҖҒжң¬еҸӨеўігҒҜгҒқгҒ®гҒҶгҒЎгҒ®1гҒӨгҒ«дҪҚзҪ®гҒҘгҒ‘гӮүгӮҢгӮӢгҖҺеӨ§ең’йҒәи·ЎзҷәжҺҳиӘҝжҹ»е ұе‘Ҡжӣё -еңҹең°еҢәз”»ж•ҙзҗҶдәӢжҘӯгҒ«дјҙгҒҶи‘ӣгҒ®и‘үең°еҢәгҒ®иӘҝжҹ»-гҖҸ е’ҢжіүеёӮж•ҷиӮІе§”е“ЎдјҡгҖҒ2014е№ҙгҖҒpp. 1-4гҖӮгҖӮ еҸӨеўіеҹҹгҒҜ[[2003е№ҙ]]пјҲ[[е№іжҲҗ]]15е№ҙпјүгҒ«е’ҢжіүеёӮжҢҮе®ҡеҸІи·ЎгҒ«жҢҮе®ҡгҒ•гӮҢгҒҰгҒ„гӮӢгҖӮ File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|еўідёҳе…ЁжҷҜ
{{small|еҸігҒ«еҫҢеҶҶйғЁгҖҒе·ҰгҒ«еүҚж–№йғЁгҖӮ}} == ж–ҮеҢ–иІЎ == === е’ҢжіүеёӮжҢҮе®ҡж–ҮеҢ–иІЎ === * еҸІи·Ў ** дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі - 2003е№ҙпјҲе№іжҲҗ15е№ҙпјү3жңҲ28ж—ҘжҢҮе®ҡ{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf е’ҢжіүеёӮгҒ®жҢҮе®ҡж–ҮеҢ–иІЎ]}}пјҲе’ҢжіүеёӮгғӣгғјгғ гғҡгғјгӮёпјүгҖӮгҖӮ == и„ҡжіЁ == {{и„ҡжіЁгғҳгғ«гғ—}} {{reflist}} == еҸӮиҖғж–ҮзҢ® == * еҸІи·ЎиӘ¬жҳҺжқҝпјҲе’ҢжіүеёӮж•ҷиӮІе§”е“ЎдјҡгҖҒ2005е№ҙиЁӯзҪ®пјү * {{Cite book|е’Ңжӣё|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=иІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі|title=[[ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»]] 28 еӨ§йҳӘеәңгҒ®ең°еҗҚ|publisher=[[е№іеҮЎзӨҫ]]|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|иІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўіпјҲе№іеҮЎзӨҫпјү|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|е’Ңжӣё|editor=|author=еӨ§и°·зҢӣ|year=1989|chapter=иІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі|title=[[ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё]]|publisher=[[жқұдә¬е ӮеҮәзүҲ]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|иІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўіпјҲеҸӨеўіпјү|1989}}}} == еӨ–йғЁгғӘгғігӮҜ == * [https://izbun.jp/introduction/shinoda/shinodakaibukiyamakofun.html дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі] - е’ҢжіүеёӮж•ҷиӮІе§”е“Ўдјҡз”ҹж¶ҜеӯҰзҝ’йғЁж–ҮеҢ–иІЎжҢҜиҲҲиӘІгҖҢе’ҢжіүеёӮгҒ®ж–ҮеҢ–иІЎгҖҚ {{гғҮгғ•гӮ©гғ«гғҲгӮҪгғјгғҲ:гҒ—гҒ®гҒҹгҒӢгҒ„гҒөгҒҚгӮ„гҒҫгҒ“гҒөгӮ“}} [[Category:еӨ§йҳӘеәңгҒ®еҸӨеўі]] [[Category:е’ҢжіүеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ]] [[Category:еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі]]

еҸӨеўі |гғ©гғҷгғ«дҪҚзҪ® = top }} '''дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі'''пјҲгҒ—гҒ®гҒ гҒӢгҒ„гҒ¶гҒҚгӮ„гҒҫгҒ“гҒөгӮ“пјүгҒҜгҖҒ[[еӨ§йҳӘеәң]][[е’ҢжіүеёӮ]]еӨӘз”әгҒ«гҒӮгӮӢ[[еҸӨеўі]]гҖӮеҪўзҠ¶гҒҜ[[еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі]]гҖӮе’ҢжіүеёӮжҢҮе®ҡеҸІи·ЎгҒ«жҢҮе®ҡгҒ•гӮҢгҒҰгҒ„гӮӢгҖӮ == жҰӮиҰҒ ==

еӨ§йҳӘеәңеҚ—йғЁгҖҒдҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷөеҢ—иҘҝйә“гҒ®дҪҺдҪҚж®өдёҳдёҠгҒ«зҜүйҖ гҒ•гӮҢгҒҹеҸӨеўігҒ§гҒӮгӮӢгҖӮгҖҢиІқеҗ№еұұгҖҚгҒ®еҸӨеўіеҗҚгҒҜгҖҒ[[жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј]]гҒ®дёҖжҸҶпјҲеҚғеҺҹйЁ’еӢ•пјүгҒ«гҒҠгҒ„гҒҰиҫІж°‘гҒҢжі•иһәиІқгӮ’еҗҲеӣігҒ«еҪ“ең°гҒ«йӣҶгҒҫгҒЈгҒҹгҒ“гҒЁгҒ«з”ұжқҘгҒҷгӮӢеҸІи·ЎиӘ¬жҳҺжқҝгҖӮгҖӮгҒ“гӮҢгҒҫгҒ§гҒ«йғЁеҲҶзҡ„гҒӘзҷәжҺҳиӘҝжҹ»гҒҢе®ҹж–ҪгҒ•гӮҢгҒҰгҒ„гӮӢ{{Sfn|иІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўіпјҲеҸӨеўіпјү|1989}}гҖӮ еўіеҪўгҒҜеүҚж–№йғЁгҒҢзҹӯе°ҸгҒӘеёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўгҒ®еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеҪўгҒ§гҖҒеүҚж–№йғЁгӮ’еҢ—иҘҝж–№гҒ«еҗ‘гҒ‘гӮӢгҖӮеўідёҳй•·гҒҜзҙ„60гғЎгғјгғҲгғ«гҖҒеҫҢеҶҶйғЁгҒҜзӣҙеҫ„50гғЎгғјгғҲгғ«гғ»й«ҳгҒ•7.7гғЎгғјгғҲгғ«гӮ’жё¬гӮӢгҖӮеўідёҳиЎЁйқўгҒ§гҒҜ[[и‘әзҹі]]гғ»[[еҹҙијӘ]]пјҲ[[еҶҶзӯ’еҹҙијӘ]]гғ»[[еҪўиұЎеҹҙијӘ]]пјүгҒҢиӘҚгӮҒгӮүгӮҢгӮӢгҒ»гҒӢгҖҒеўідёҳе‘ЁеӣІгҒ«гҒҜе‘Ёжҝ гҒҢе·ЎгӮүгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢ{{Sfn|иІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўіпјҲеҸӨеўіпјү|1989}}гҖӮдё»дҪ“йғЁгҒ®еҹӢи‘¬ж–ҪиЁӯгҒҜжҳҺгӮүгҒӢгҒ§гҒӘгҒ„гҖӮеҮәеңҹе“ҒгҒ«гҒҜеҹҙијӘгҒ®гҒ»гҒӢж»‘зҹіиЈҪж§ҪгҒҢгҒӮгӮӢ{{Sfn|иІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўіпјҲеҸӨеўіпјү|1989}}гҖӮ гҒ“гҒ®дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўігҒҜгҖҒ[[еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј]]дёӯжңҹгҒ®[[5дё–зҙҖ]]еүҚеҚҠй ғгҒ®зҜүйҖ гҒЁжҺЁе®ҡгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢгҖӮе’Ңжіүең°ж–№еҢ—йғЁгҒ§гҒҜйҰ–й•·еў“еҸӨеўігҒЁгҒ—гҒҰеҢ—зҫӨгғ»еҚ—зҫӨгҒ®2зҫӨгҒҢиӘҚгӮҒгӮүгӮҢгӮӢгҒҢгҖҒеҢ—зҫӨгғ»еҚ—зҫӨгҒ«гҒҜгҒқгӮҢгҒһгӮҢжҷӮжңҹзҡ„з©әзҷҪгҒҢиӘҚгӮҒгӮүгӮҢгӮӢгҒ“гҒЁгҒӢгӮүдёЎзҫӨгҒҢијӘз•ӘеҲ¶гҒ§йҰ–й•·гӮ’жӢ…гҒЈгҒҹгҒЁжҺЁе®ҡгҒ•гӮҢгҖҒжң¬еҸӨеўігҒҜгҒқгҒ®гҒҶгҒЎгҒ®1гҒӨгҒ«дҪҚзҪ®гҒҘгҒ‘гӮүгӮҢгӮӢгҖҺеӨ§ең’йҒәи·ЎзҷәжҺҳиӘҝжҹ»е ұе‘Ҡжӣё -еңҹең°еҢәз”»ж•ҙзҗҶдәӢжҘӯгҒ«дјҙгҒҶи‘ӣгҒ®и‘үең°еҢәгҒ®иӘҝжҹ»-гҖҸ е’ҢжіүеёӮж•ҷиӮІе§”е“ЎдјҡгҖҒ2014е№ҙгҖҒpp. 1-4гҖӮгҖӮ еҸӨеўіеҹҹгҒҜ[[2003е№ҙ]]пјҲ[[е№іжҲҗ]]15е№ҙпјүгҒ«е’ҢжіүеёӮжҢҮе®ҡеҸІи·ЎгҒ«жҢҮе®ҡгҒ•гӮҢгҒҰгҒ„гӮӢгҖӮ File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|еўідёҳе…ЁжҷҜ
{{small|еҸігҒ«еҫҢеҶҶйғЁгҖҒе·ҰгҒ«еүҚж–№йғЁгҖӮ}} == ж–ҮеҢ–иІЎ == === е’ҢжіүеёӮжҢҮе®ҡж–ҮеҢ–иІЎ === * еҸІи·Ў ** дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі - 2003е№ҙпјҲе№іжҲҗ15е№ҙпјү3жңҲ28ж—ҘжҢҮе®ҡ{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf е’ҢжіүеёӮгҒ®жҢҮе®ҡж–ҮеҢ–иІЎ]}}пјҲе’ҢжіүеёӮгғӣгғјгғ гғҡгғјгӮёпјүгҖӮгҖӮ == и„ҡжіЁ == {{и„ҡжіЁгғҳгғ«гғ—}} {{reflist}} == еҸӮиҖғж–ҮзҢ® == * еҸІи·ЎиӘ¬жҳҺжқҝпјҲе’ҢжіүеёӮж•ҷиӮІе§”е“ЎдјҡгҖҒ2005е№ҙиЁӯзҪ®пјү * {{Cite book|е’Ңжӣё|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=иІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі|title=[[ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»]] 28 еӨ§йҳӘеәңгҒ®ең°еҗҚ|publisher=[[е№іеҮЎзӨҫ]]|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|иІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўіпјҲе№іеҮЎзӨҫпјү|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|е’Ңжӣё|editor=|author=еӨ§и°·зҢӣ|year=1989|chapter=иІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі|title=[[ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё]]|publisher=[[жқұдә¬е ӮеҮәзүҲ]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|иІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўіпјҲеҸӨеўіпјү|1989}}}} == еӨ–йғЁгғӘгғігӮҜ == {{Commonscat|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun}} * [https://izbun.jp/introduction/shinoda/shinodakaibukiyamakofun.html дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі] - е’ҢжіүеёӮж•ҷиӮІе§”е“Ўдјҡз”ҹж¶ҜеӯҰзҝ’йғЁж–ҮеҢ–иІЎжҢҜиҲҲиӘІгҖҢе’ҢжіүеёӮгҒ®ж–ҮеҢ–иІЎгҖҚ {{гғҮгғ•гӮ©гғ«гғҲгӮҪгғјгғҲ:гҒ—гҒ®гҒҹгҒӢгҒ„гҒөгҒҚгӮ„гҒҫгҒ“гҒөгӮ“}} [[Category:еӨ§йҳӘеәңгҒ®еҸӨеўі]] [[Category:е’ҢжіүеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ]] [[Category:еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі]]

еҸӨеўі |гғ©гғҷгғ«дҪҚзҪ® = top }} '''дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі'''пјҲгҒ—гҒ®гҒ гҒӢгҒ„гҒ¶гҒҚгӮ„гҒҫгҒ“гҒөгӮ“пјүгҒҜгҖҒ[[еӨ§йҳӘеәң]][[е’ҢжіүеёӮ]]еӨӘз”әгҒ«гҒӮгӮӢ[[еҸӨеўі]]гҖӮеҪўзҠ¶гҒҜ[[еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі]]гҖӮе’ҢжіүеёӮжҢҮе®ҡеҸІи·ЎгҒ«жҢҮе®ҡгҒ•гӮҢгҒҰгҒ„гӮӢгҖӮ == жҰӮиҰҒ ==

еӨ§йҳӘеәңеҚ—йғЁгҖҒдҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷөеҢ—иҘҝйә“гҒ®дҪҺдҪҚж®өдёҳдёҠгҒ«зҜүйҖ гҒ•гӮҢгҒҹеҸӨеўігҒ§гҒӮгӮӢгҖӮгҖҢиІқеҗ№еұұгҖҚгҒ®еҸӨеўіеҗҚгҒҜгҖҒ[[жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј]]гҒ®дёҖжҸҶпјҲеҚғеҺҹйЁ’еӢ•пјүгҒ«гҒҠгҒ„гҒҰиҫІж°‘гҒҢжі•иһәиІқгӮ’еҗҲеӣігҒ«еҪ“ең°гҒ«йӣҶгҒҫгҒЈгҒҹгҒ“гҒЁгҒ«з”ұжқҘгҒҷгӮӢеҸІи·ЎиӘ¬жҳҺжқҝгҖӮгҖӮгҒ“гӮҢгҒҫгҒ§гҒ«йғЁеҲҶзҡ„гҒӘзҷәжҺҳиӘҝжҹ»гҒҢе®ҹж–ҪгҒ•гӮҢгҒҰгҒ„гӮӢ{{Sfn|иІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўіпјҲеҸӨеўіпјү|1989}}гҖӮ еўіеҪўгҒҜеүҚж–№йғЁгҒҢзҹӯе°ҸгҒӘеёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўгҒ®еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеҪўгҒ§гҖҒеүҚж–№йғЁгӮ’еҢ—иҘҝж–№гҒ«еҗ‘гҒ‘гӮӢгҖӮеўідёҳй•·гҒҜзҙ„60гғЎгғјгғҲгғ«гҖҒеҫҢеҶҶйғЁгҒҜзӣҙеҫ„50гғЎгғјгғҲгғ«гғ»й«ҳгҒ•7.7гғЎгғјгғҲгғ«гӮ’жё¬гӮӢгҖӮеўідёҳиЎЁйқўгҒ§гҒҜ[[и‘әзҹі]]гғ»[[еҹҙијӘ]]пјҲ[[еҶҶзӯ’еҹҙијӘ]]гғ»[[еҪўиұЎеҹҙијӘ]]пјүгҒҢиӘҚгӮҒгӮүгӮҢгӮӢгҒ»гҒӢгҖҒеўідёҳе‘ЁеӣІгҒ«гҒҜе‘Ёжҝ гҒҢе·ЎгӮүгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢ{{Sfn|иІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўіпјҲеҸӨеўіпјү|1989}}гҖӮдё»дҪ“йғЁгҒ®еҹӢи‘¬ж–ҪиЁӯгҒҜжҳҺгӮүгҒӢгҒ§гҒӘгҒ„гҖӮеҮәеңҹе“ҒгҒ«гҒҜеҹҙијӘгҒ®гҒ»гҒӢж»‘зҹіиЈҪж§ҪгҒҢгҒӮгӮӢ{{Sfn|иІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўіпјҲеҸӨеўіпјү|1989}}гҖӮ гҒ“гҒ®дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўігҒҜгҖҒ[[еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј]]дёӯжңҹгҒ®[[5дё–зҙҖ]]еүҚеҚҠй ғгҒ®зҜүйҖ гҒЁжҺЁе®ҡгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢгҖӮе’Ңжіүең°ж–№еҢ—йғЁгҒ§гҒҜйҰ–й•·еў“еҸӨеўігҒЁгҒ—гҒҰеҢ—зҫӨгғ»еҚ—зҫӨгҒ®2зҫӨгҒҢиӘҚгӮҒгӮүгӮҢгӮӢгҒҢгҖҒеҢ—зҫӨгғ»еҚ—зҫӨгҒ«гҒҜгҒқгӮҢгҒһгӮҢжҷӮжңҹзҡ„з©әзҷҪгҒҢиӘҚгӮҒгӮүгӮҢгӮӢгҒ“гҒЁгҒӢгӮүдёЎзҫӨгҒҢијӘз•ӘеҲ¶гҒ§йҰ–й•·гӮ’жӢ…гҒЈгҒҹгҒЁжҺЁе®ҡгҒ•гӮҢгҖҒжң¬еҸӨеўігҒҜгҒқгҒ®гҒҶгҒЎгҒ®1гҒӨгҒ«дҪҚзҪ®гҒҘгҒ‘гӮүгӮҢгӮӢгҖҺеӨ§ең’йҒәи·ЎзҷәжҺҳиӘҝжҹ»е ұе‘Ҡжӣё -еңҹең°еҢәз”»ж•ҙзҗҶдәӢжҘӯгҒ«дјҙгҒҶи‘ӣгҒ®и‘үең°еҢәгҒ®иӘҝжҹ»-гҖҸ е’ҢжіүеёӮж•ҷиӮІе§”е“ЎдјҡгҖҒ2014е№ҙгҖҒpp. 1-4гҖӮгҖӮ еҸӨеўіеҹҹгҒҜ[[2003е№ҙ]]пјҲ[[е№іжҲҗ]]15е№ҙпјүгҒ«е’ҢжіүеёӮжҢҮе®ҡеҸІи·ЎгҒ«жҢҮе®ҡгҒ•гӮҢгҒҰгҒ„гӮӢгҖӮ File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|еўідёҳе…ЁжҷҜ
{{small|дёӯеӨ®гҒ«еҫҢеҶҶйғЁгҖҒе·ҰгҒ«еүҚж–№йғЁгҖӮ}} == ж–ҮеҢ–иІЎ == === е’ҢжіүеёӮжҢҮе®ҡж–ҮеҢ–иІЎ === * еҸІи·Ў ** дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі - 2003е№ҙпјҲе№іжҲҗ15е№ҙпјү3жңҲ28ж—ҘжҢҮе®ҡ{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf е’ҢжіүеёӮгҒ®жҢҮе®ҡж–ҮеҢ–иІЎ]}}пјҲе’ҢжіүеёӮгғӣгғјгғ гғҡгғјгӮёпјүгҖӮгҖӮ == и„ҡжіЁ == {{и„ҡжіЁгғҳгғ«гғ—}} {{reflist}} == еҸӮиҖғж–ҮзҢ® == * еҸІи·ЎиӘ¬жҳҺжқҝпјҲе’ҢжіүеёӮж•ҷиӮІе§”е“ЎдјҡгҖҒ2005е№ҙиЁӯзҪ®пјү * {{Cite book|е’Ңжӣё|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=иІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі|title=[[ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»]] 28 еӨ§йҳӘеәңгҒ®ең°еҗҚ|publisher=[[е№іеҮЎзӨҫ]]|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|иІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўіпјҲе№іеҮЎзӨҫпјү|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|е’Ңжӣё|editor=|author=еӨ§и°·зҢӣ|year=1989|chapter=иІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі|title=[[ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё]]|publisher=[[жқұдә¬е ӮеҮәзүҲ]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|иІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўіпјҲеҸӨеўіпјү|1989}}}} == еӨ–йғЁгғӘгғігӮҜ == {{Commonscat|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun}} * [https://izbun.jp/introduction/shinoda/shinodakaibukiyamakofun.html дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі] - е’ҢжіүеёӮж•ҷиӮІе§”е“Ўдјҡз”ҹж¶ҜеӯҰзҝ’йғЁж–ҮеҢ–иІЎжҢҜиҲҲиӘІгҖҢе’ҢжіүеёӮгҒ®ж–ҮеҢ–иІЎгҖҚ {{гғҮгғ•гӮ©гғ«гғҲгӮҪгғјгғҲ:гҒ—гҒ®гҒҹгҒӢгҒ„гҒөгҒҚгӮ„гҒҫгҒ“гҒөгӮ“}} [[Category:еӨ§йҳӘеәңгҒ®еҸӨеўі]] [[Category:е’ҢжіүеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ]] [[Category:еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі]]

еҸӨеўі |гғ©гғҷгғ«дҪҚзҪ® = top }} '''дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі'''пјҲгҒ—гҒ®гҒ гҒӢгҒ„гҒ¶гҒҚгӮ„гҒҫгҒ“гҒөгӮ“пјүгҒҜгҖҒ[[еӨ§йҳӘеәң]][[е’ҢжіүеёӮ]]еӨӘз”әгҒ«гҒӮгӮӢ[[еҸӨеўі]]гҖӮеҪўзҠ¶гҒҜ[[еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі]]гҖӮе’ҢжіүеёӮжҢҮе®ҡеҸІи·ЎгҒ«жҢҮе®ҡгҒ•гӮҢгҒҰгҒ„гӮӢгҖӮ == жҰӮиҰҒ ==

еӨ§йҳӘеәңеҚ—йғЁгҖҒдҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷөеҢ—иҘҝйә“гҒ®дҪҺдҪҚж®өдёҳдёҠгҒ«зҜүйҖ гҒ•гӮҢгҒҹеҸӨеўігҒ§гҒӮгӮӢгҖӮгҖҢиІқеҗ№еұұгҖҚгҒ®еҸӨеўіеҗҚгҒҜгҖҒ[[жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј]]гҒ®дёҖжҸҶпјҲеҚғеҺҹйЁ’еӢ•пјүгҒ«гҒҠгҒ„гҒҰиҫІж°‘гҒҢжі•иһәиІқгӮ’еҗҲеӣігҒ«еҪ“ең°гҒ«йӣҶгҒҫгҒЈгҒҹгҒ“гҒЁгҒ«з”ұжқҘгҒҷгӮӢеҸІи·ЎиӘ¬жҳҺжқҝгҖӮгҖӮгҒ“гӮҢгҒҫгҒ§гҒ«йғЁеҲҶзҡ„гҒӘзҷәжҺҳиӘҝжҹ»гҒҢе®ҹж–ҪгҒ•гӮҢгҒҰгҒ„гӮӢ{{Sfn|иІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўіпјҲеҸӨеўіпјү|1989}}гҖӮ еўіеҪўгҒҜеүҚж–№йғЁгҒҢзҹӯе°ҸгҒӘеёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўгҒ®еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеҪўгҒ§гҖҒеүҚж–№йғЁгӮ’еҢ—иҘҝж–№гҒ«еҗ‘гҒ‘гӮӢгҖӮеўідёҳй•·гҒҜзҙ„60гғЎгғјгғҲгғ«гҖҒеҫҢеҶҶйғЁгҒҜзӣҙеҫ„50гғЎгғјгғҲгғ«гғ»й«ҳгҒ•7.7гғЎгғјгғҲгғ«гӮ’жё¬гӮӢгҖӮеўідёҳиЎЁйқўгҒ§гҒҜ[[и‘әзҹі]]гғ»[[еҹҙијӘ]]пјҲ[[еҶҶзӯ’еҹҙијӘ]]гғ»[[еҪўиұЎеҹҙијӘ]]пјүгҒҢиӘҚгӮҒгӮүгӮҢгӮӢгҒ»гҒӢгҖҒеўідёҳе‘ЁеӣІгҒ«гҒҜе‘Ёжҝ гҒҢе·ЎгӮүгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢ{{Sfn|иІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўіпјҲеҸӨеўіпјү|1989}}гҖӮеҹӢи‘¬ж–ҪиЁӯгҒҜжҳҺгӮүгҒӢгҒ§гҒӘгҒ„гҖӮеҮәеңҹе“ҒгҒ«гҒҜеҹҙијӘгҒ®гҒ»гҒӢж»‘зҹіиЈҪж§ҪгҒҢгҒӮгӮӢ{{Sfn|иІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўіпјҲеҸӨеўіпјү|1989}}гҖӮ гҒ“гҒ®дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўігҒҜгҖҒ[[еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј]]дёӯжңҹгҒ®[[5дё–зҙҖ]]еүҚеҚҠй ғгҒ®зҜүйҖ гҒЁжҺЁе®ҡгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢгҖӮе’Ңжіүең°ж–№еҢ—йғЁгҒ§гҒҜйҰ–й•·еў“еҸӨеўігҒЁгҒ—гҒҰеҢ—зҫӨгғ»еҚ—зҫӨгҒ®2зҫӨгҒҢиӘҚгӮҒгӮүгӮҢгӮӢгҒҢгҖҒеҢ—зҫӨгғ»еҚ—зҫӨгҒ«гҒҜгҒқгӮҢгҒһгӮҢжҷӮжңҹзҡ„з©әзҷҪгҒҢиӘҚгӮҒгӮүгӮҢгӮӢгҒ“гҒЁгҒӢгӮүдёЎзҫӨгҒҢијӘз•ӘеҲ¶гҒ§йҰ–й•·гӮ’жӢ…гҒЈгҒҹгҒЁжҺЁе®ҡгҒ•гӮҢгҖҒжң¬еҸӨеўігҒҜгҒқгҒ®гҒҶгҒЎгҒ®1гҒӨгҒ«дҪҚзҪ®гҒҘгҒ‘гӮүгӮҢгӮӢгҖҺеӨ§ең’йҒәи·ЎзҷәжҺҳиӘҝжҹ»е ұе‘Ҡжӣё -еңҹең°еҢәз”»ж•ҙзҗҶдәӢжҘӯгҒ«дјҙгҒҶи‘ӣгҒ®и‘үең°еҢәгҒ®иӘҝжҹ»-гҖҸ е’ҢжіүеёӮж•ҷиӮІе§”е“ЎдјҡгҖҒ2014е№ҙгҖҒpp. 1-4гҖӮгҖӮ еҸӨеўіеҹҹгҒҜ[[2003е№ҙ]]пјҲ[[е№іжҲҗ]]15е№ҙпјүгҒ«е’ҢжіүеёӮжҢҮе®ҡеҸІи·ЎгҒ«жҢҮе®ҡгҒ•гӮҢгҒҰгҒ„гӮӢгҖӮ File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|еўідёҳе…ЁжҷҜ
{{small|дёӯеӨ®гҒ«еҫҢеҶҶйғЁгҖҒе·ҰгҒ«еүҚж–№йғЁгҖӮ}} == ж–ҮеҢ–иІЎ == === е’ҢжіүеёӮжҢҮе®ҡж–ҮеҢ–иІЎ === * еҸІи·Ў ** дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі - 2003е№ҙпјҲе№іжҲҗ15е№ҙпјү3жңҲ28ж—ҘжҢҮе®ҡ{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf е’ҢжіүеёӮгҒ®жҢҮе®ҡж–ҮеҢ–иІЎ]}}пјҲе’ҢжіүеёӮгғӣгғјгғ гғҡгғјгӮёпјүгҖӮгҖӮ == и„ҡжіЁ == {{и„ҡжіЁгғҳгғ«гғ—}} {{reflist}} == еҸӮиҖғж–ҮзҢ® == * еҸІи·ЎиӘ¬жҳҺжқҝпјҲе’ҢжіүеёӮж•ҷиӮІе§”е“ЎдјҡгҖҒ2005е№ҙиЁӯзҪ®пјү * {{Cite book|е’Ңжӣё|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=иІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі|title=[[ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»]] 28 еӨ§йҳӘеәңгҒ®ең°еҗҚ|publisher=[[е№іеҮЎзӨҫ]]|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|иІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўіпјҲе№іеҮЎзӨҫпјү|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|е’Ңжӣё|editor=|author=еӨ§и°·зҢӣ|year=1989|chapter=иІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі|title=[[ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё]]|publisher=[[жқұдә¬е ӮеҮәзүҲ]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|иІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўіпјҲеҸӨеўіпјү|1989}}}} == еӨ–йғЁгғӘгғігӮҜ == {{Commonscat|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun}} * [https://izbun.jp/introduction/shinoda/shinodakaibukiyamakofun.html дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі] - е’ҢжіүеёӮж•ҷиӮІе§”е“Ўдјҡз”ҹж¶ҜеӯҰзҝ’йғЁж–ҮеҢ–иІЎжҢҜиҲҲиӘІгҖҢе’ҢжіүеёӮгҒ®ж–ҮеҢ–иІЎгҖҚ {{гғҮгғ•гӮ©гғ«гғҲгӮҪгғјгғҲ:гҒ—гҒ®гҒҹгҒӢгҒ„гҒөгҒҚгӮ„гҒҫгҒ“гҒөгӮ“}} [[Category:еӨ§йҳӘеәңгҒ®еҸӨеўі]] [[Category:е’ҢжіүеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ]] [[Category:еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі]]

еҸӨеўі |гғ©гғҷгғ«дҪҚзҪ® = top }} '''дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі'''пјҲгҒ—гҒ®гҒ гҒӢгҒ„гҒ¶гҒҚгӮ„гҒҫгҒ“гҒөгӮ“пјүгҒҜгҖҒ[[еӨ§йҳӘеәң]][[е’ҢжіүеёӮ]]еӨӘз”әгҒ«гҒӮгӮӢ[[еҸӨеўі]]гҖӮеҪўзҠ¶гҒҜ[[еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі]]гҖӮе’ҢжіүеёӮжҢҮе®ҡеҸІи·ЎгҒ«жҢҮе®ҡгҒ•гӮҢгҒҰгҒ„гӮӢгҖӮ == жҰӮиҰҒ ==

еӨ§йҳӘеәңеҚ—йғЁгҖҒдҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷөеҢ—иҘҝйә“гҒ®дҪҺдҪҚж®өдёҳдёҠгҒ«зҜүйҖ гҒ•гӮҢгҒҹеҸӨеўігҒ§гҒӮгӮӢгҖӮгҖҢиІқеҗ№еұұгҖҚгҒ®еҸӨеўіеҗҚгҒҜгҖҒ[[жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј]]гҒ®дёҖжҸҶпјҲеҚғеҺҹйЁ’еӢ•пјүгҒ«гҒҠгҒ„гҒҰиҫІж°‘гҒҢжі•иһәиІқгӮ’еҗҲеӣігҒ«еҪ“ең°гҒ«йӣҶгҒҫгҒЈгҒҹгҒ“гҒЁгҒ«з”ұжқҘгҒҷгӮӢеҸІи·ЎиӘ¬жҳҺжқҝгҖӮгҖӮгҒ“гӮҢгҒҫгҒ§гҒ«йғЁеҲҶзҡ„гҒӘзҷәжҺҳиӘҝжҹ»гҒҢе®ҹж–ҪгҒ•гӮҢгҒҰгҒ„гӮӢ{{Sfn|иІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўіпјҲеҸӨеўіпјү|1989}}гҖӮ еўіеҪўгҒҜеүҚж–№йғЁгҒҢзҹӯе°ҸгҒӘеёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўгҒ®еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеҪўгҒ§гҖҒеүҚж–№йғЁгӮ’еҢ—иҘҝж–№еҗ‘гҒ«еҗ‘гҒ‘гӮӢгҖӮеўідёҳй•·гҒҜзҙ„60гғЎгғјгғҲгғ«гҖҒеҫҢеҶҶйғЁгҒҜзӣҙеҫ„50гғЎгғјгғҲгғ«гғ»й«ҳгҒ•7.7гғЎгғјгғҲгғ«гӮ’жё¬гӮӢгҖӮеўідёҳиЎЁйқўгҒ§гҒҜ[[и‘әзҹі]]гғ»[[еҹҙијӘ]]пјҲ[[еҶҶзӯ’еҹҙијӘ]]гғ»[[еҪўиұЎеҹҙијӘ]]пјүгҒҢиӘҚгӮҒгӮүгӮҢгӮӢгҒ»гҒӢгҖҒеўідёҳе‘ЁеӣІгҒ«гҒҜе‘Ёжҝ гҒҢе·ЎгӮүгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢ{{Sfn|иІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўіпјҲеҸӨеўіпјү|1989}}гҖӮеҹӢи‘¬ж–ҪиЁӯгҒҜжҳҺгӮүгҒӢгҒ§гҒӘгҒ„гҖӮеҮәеңҹе“ҒгҒ«гҒҜеҹҙијӘгҒ®гҒ»гҒӢж»‘зҹіиЈҪж§ҪгҒҢгҒӮгӮӢ{{Sfn|иІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўіпјҲеҸӨеўіпјү|1989}}гҖӮ зҜүйҖ жҷӮжңҹгҒҜгҖҒ[[еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј]]дёӯжңҹгҒ®[[5дё–зҙҖ]]еүҚеҚҠй ғгҒЁжҺЁе®ҡгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢгҖӮе’Ңжіүең°ж–№еҢ—йғЁгҒ§гҒҜйҰ–й•·еў“еҸӨеўігҒЁгҒ—гҒҰеҢ—зҫӨгғ»еҚ—зҫӨгҒ®2зҫӨгҒҢиӘҚгӮҒгӮүгӮҢгӮӢгҒҢгҖҒеҢ—зҫӨгғ»еҚ—зҫӨгҒ«гҒҜгҒқгӮҢгҒһгӮҢжҷӮжңҹзҡ„з©әзҷҪгҒҢиӘҚгӮҒгӮүгӮҢгӮӢгҒ“гҒЁгҒӢгӮүдёЎзҫӨгҒҢијӘз•ӘеҲ¶гҒ§йҰ–й•·гӮ’жӢ…гҒЈгҒҹгҒЁжҺЁе®ҡгҒ•гӮҢгҖҒжң¬еҸӨеўігҒҜгҒқгҒ®гҒҶгҒЎгҒ®1гҒӨгҒ«дҪҚзҪ®гҒҘгҒ‘гӮүгӮҢгӮӢгҖҺеӨ§ең’йҒәи·ЎзҷәжҺҳиӘҝжҹ»е ұе‘Ҡжӣё -еңҹең°еҢәз”»ж•ҙзҗҶдәӢжҘӯгҒ«дјҙгҒҶи‘ӣгҒ®и‘үең°еҢәгҒ®иӘҝжҹ»-гҖҸ е’ҢжіүеёӮж•ҷиӮІе§”е“ЎдјҡгҖҒ2014е№ҙгҖҒpp. 1-4гҖӮгҖӮ еҸӨеўіеҹҹгҒҜ[[2003е№ҙ]]пјҲ[[е№іжҲҗ]]15е№ҙпјүгҒ«е’ҢжіүеёӮжҢҮе®ҡеҸІи·ЎгҒ«жҢҮе®ҡгҒ•гӮҢгҒҰгҒ„гӮӢгҖӮ File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|еўідёҳе…ЁжҷҜ
{{small|дёӯеӨ®гҒ«еҫҢеҶҶйғЁгҖҒе·ҰгҒ«еүҚж–№йғЁгҖӮ}} == ж–ҮеҢ–иІЎ == === е’ҢжіүеёӮжҢҮе®ҡж–ҮеҢ–иІЎ === * еҸІи·Ў ** дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі - 2003е№ҙпјҲе№іжҲҗ15е№ҙпјү3жңҲ28ж—ҘжҢҮе®ҡ{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf е’ҢжіүеёӮгҒ®жҢҮе®ҡж–ҮеҢ–иІЎ]}}пјҲе’ҢжіүеёӮгғӣгғјгғ гғҡгғјгӮёпјүгҖӮгҖӮ == и„ҡжіЁ == {{и„ҡжіЁгғҳгғ«гғ—}} {{reflist}} == еҸӮиҖғж–ҮзҢ® == {{small|пјҲиЁҳдәӢеҹ·зӯҶгҒ«дҪҝз”ЁгҒ—гҒҹж–ҮзҢ®пјү}} * еҸІи·ЎиӘ¬жҳҺжқҝпјҲе’ҢжіүеёӮж•ҷиӮІе§”е“ЎдјҡгҖҒ2005е№ҙиЁӯзҪ®пјү * {{Cite book|е’Ңжӣё|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=иІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі|title=[[ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»]] 28 еӨ§йҳӘеәңгҒ®ең°еҗҚ|publisher=[[е№іеҮЎзӨҫ]]|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|иІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўіпјҲе№іеҮЎзӨҫпјү|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|е’Ңжӣё|editor=|author=еӨ§и°·зҢӣ|year=1989|chapter=иІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі|title=[[ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё]]|publisher=[[жқұдә¬е ӮеҮәзүҲ]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|иІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўіпјҲеҸӨеўіпјү|1989}}}} == й–ўйҖЈж–ҮзҢ® == {{small|пјҲиЁҳдәӢеҹ·зӯҶгҒ«дҪҝз”ЁгҒ—гҒҰгҒ„гҒӘгҒ„й–ўйҖЈж–ҮзҢ®пјү}} * {{Cite book|е’Ңжӣё|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі|title=е’ҢжіүеёӮеҹӢи”өж–ҮеҢ–иІЎзҷәжҺҳиӘҝжҹ»жҰӮе ұ26|publisher=е’ҢжіүеёӮж•ҷиӮІе§”е“Ўдјҡ|isbn=|ref=}} == еӨ–йғЁгғӘгғігӮҜ == {{Commonscat|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun}} * [https://izbun.jp/introduction/shinoda/shinodakaibukiyamakofun.html дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі] - е’ҢжіүеёӮж•ҷиӮІе§”е“Ўдјҡз”ҹж¶ҜеӯҰзҝ’йғЁж–ҮеҢ–иІЎжҢҜиҲҲиӘІгҖҢе’ҢжіүеёӮгҒ®ж–ҮеҢ–иІЎгҖҚ {{гғҮгғ•гӮ©гғ«гғҲгӮҪгғјгғҲ:гҒ—гҒ®гҒҹгҒӢгҒ„гҒөгҒҚгӮ„гҒҫгҒ“гҒөгӮ“}} [[Category:еӨ§йҳӘеәңгҒ®еҸӨеўі]] [[Category:е’ҢжіүеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ]] [[Category:еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі]]

revision.text:
{{Kofun of Japan |Name = Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun |Location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|еӨ§йҳӘеәң}} {{ill|Izumi, Osaka|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮ}} TaichЕҚ |Coordinates = {{coord|34|30|30.40|N|135|26|32.25|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun}} |Type = Scallop-shaped Kofun |Size = Mound length: 60m, height: 7.7m |Burial Facilities = Unknown |Artifacts = Steatite trough, {{ill|haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments |Accompanying Burial Mounds = None |Era = Early 5th century |Burial = Unknown |Historic Designation = Izumi City Designated Historic Site (Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun) |Image = [[File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the mound and information plaque }} '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' (дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|еҸӨеўі}} located in {{ill|Izumi, Osaka|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮ}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|еӨ§йҳӘеәң}}. It is a scallop-shaped tumulus designated as a historic site by Izumi City. == Overview == {{thumb|right|250px|Panoramic view of the mound, with the rear circular part at the center and the short rectangular front to the left.}} The kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwest foothills of the Shinoda Hills in southern Osaka Prefecture. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the Edo-period uprisings (Chihara Riots), during which farmers reportedly used conch shells to signal and gather at the site.Information plaque at the site, installed by Izumi City Board of Education in 2005. Partial excavations have been conducted.Kaibukiyama Kofun, Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofun (Tokyo-dЕҚ Shuppan, 1989). The mound has a scallop-shaped layout, with a short rectangular front extending northwestward. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular part having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound's surface includes {{ill|fukiishi|ja|и‘әзҹі}} and {{ill|haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} (both cylindrical and figurative types), and it is surrounded by a moat. The burial facility remains unidentified, but artifacts such as a steatite trough and haniwa fragments have been unearthed. The kofun is estimated to date back to the early 5th century during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј}}."Genealogy of Leaders in Northern Izumi," Izumi City's History Volume 6, pp. 65-70. In the northern Izumi region, the leader's burial mounds are divided into northern and southern clusters. A rotational leadership system is hypothesized based on temporal gaps between the clusters, with this kofun belonging to one such phase.Excavation Report on ЕҢzono Site (Izumi City Board of Education, 2014). The kofun was designated as a historic site by Izumi City in 2003.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf List of Designated Cultural Properties in Izumi City]}}. == Cultural Properties == === Izumi City Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun - Designated on March 28, 2003. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes Help}} == Sources == {{small|Sources used in writing this article}} * Information plaque (Izumi City Board of Education, 2005) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Kaibukiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Encyclopedia of Japanese Historical Place Names|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»}} Osaka Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|е№іеҮЎзӨҫ}}|isbn=458249028X|ref=Kaibukiyama Kofun (Heibonsha, 1986)}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Takashi ЕҢtani|year=1989|chapter=Kaibukiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-dЕҚ Shuppan|ja|жқұдә¬е ӮеҮәзүҲ}}|isbn=4490102607|ref=Kaibukiyama Kofun (Kofun, 1989)}} == Further Reading == {{small|Additional sources not used in this article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|title=Izumi City Excavation Report 26|publisher=Izumi City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun}} * [https://izbun.jp/introduction/shinoda/shinodakaibukiyamakofun.html Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun] - Cultural Property Section, Izumi City Board of Education [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Izumi, Osaka]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped Kofun]] [[ja:дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі]]

In the northern Izumi region, the leader's burial mounds are divided into northern and southern clusters. A rotational leadership system is hypothesized based on temporal gaps between the clusters, with this kofun belonging to one such phase.Excavation Report on ЕҢzono Site (Izumi City Board of Education, 2014). The kofun was designated as a historic site by Izumi City in 2003.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf List of Designated Cultural Properties in Izumi City]}}. == Cultural Properties == === Izumi City Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun - Designated on March 28, 2003. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes Help}} == Sources == {{small|Sources used in writing this article}} * Information plaque (Izumi City Board of Education, 2005) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Kaibukiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Encyclopedia of Japanese Historical Place Names|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»}} Osaka Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|е№іеҮЎзӨҫ}}|isbn=458249028X|ref=Kaibukiyama Kofun (Heibonsha, 1986)}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Takashi ЕҢtani|year=1989|chapter=Kaibukiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-dЕҚ Shuppan|ja|жқұдә¬е ӮеҮәзүҲ}}|isbn=4490102607|ref=Kaibukiyama Kofun (Kofun, 1989)}} == Further Reading == {{small|Additional sources not used in this article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|title=Izumi City Excavation Report 26|publisher=Izumi City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun}} * [https://izbun.jp/introduction/shinoda/shinodakaibukiyamakofun.html Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun] - Cultural Property Section, Izumi City Board of Education [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Izumi, Osaka]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped Kofun]] [[category:new pages]][[ja:дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі]]

{{Kofun of Japan |Name = Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|еӨ§йҳӘеәң}} {{ill|Izumi, Osaka|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮ}} TaichЕҚ |coordinates = {{coord|34|30|30.40|N|135|26|32.25|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun}} |Type = Scallop-shaped Kofun |Size = Mound length: 60m, height: 7.7m |Burial Facilities = Unknown |Artifacts = Steatite trough, {{ill|haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments |Accompanying Burial Mounds = None |Era = Early 5th century |Burial = Unknown |Historic Designation = Izumi City Designated Historic Site (Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun) |image = [[File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the mound and information plaque }} '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' (дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|еҸӨеўі}} located in {{ill|Izumi, Osaka|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮ}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|еӨ§йҳӘеәң}}. It is a scallop-shaped tumulus designated as a historic site by Izumi City. == Overview == {{thumb|right|250px|Panoramic view of the mound, with the rear circular part at the center and the short rectangular front to the left.}} The kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwest foothills of the Shinoda Hills in southern Osaka Prefecture. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the Edo-period uprisings (Chihara Riots), during which farmers reportedly used conch shells to signal and gather at the site.Information plaque at the site, installed by Izumi City Board of Education in 2005. Partial excavations have been conducted.Kaibukiyama Kofun, Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofun (Tokyo-dЕҚ Shuppan, 1989). The mound has a scallop-shaped layout, with a short rectangular front extending northwestward. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular part having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound's surface includes {{ill|fukiishi|ja|и‘әзҹі}} and {{ill|haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} (both cylindrical and figurative types), and it is surrounded by a moat. The burial facility remains unidentified, but artifacts such as a steatite trough and haniwa fragments have been unearthed. The kofun is estimated to date back to the early 5th century during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј}}."Genealogy of Leaders in Northern Izumi," Izumi City's History Volume 6, pp. 65-70. In the northern Izumi region, the leader's burial mounds are divided into northern and southern clusters. A rotational leadership system is hypothesized based on temporal gaps between the clusters, with this kofun belonging to one such phase.Excavation Report on ЕҢzono Site (Izumi City Board of Education, 2014). The kofun was designated as a historic site by Izumi City in 2003.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf List of Designated Cultural Properties in Izumi City]}}. == Cultural Properties == === Izumi City Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun - Designated on March 28, 2003. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes Help}} == Sources == {{small|Sources used in writing this article}} * Information plaque (Izumi City Board of Education, 2005) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Kaibukiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Encyclopedia of Japanese Historical Place Names|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»}} Osaka Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|е№іеҮЎзӨҫ}}|isbn=458249028X|ref=Kaibukiyama Kofun (Heibonsha, 1986)}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Takashi ЕҢtani|year=1989|chapter=Kaibukiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-dЕҚ Shuppan|ja|жқұдә¬е ӮеҮәзүҲ}}|isbn=4490102607|ref=Kaibukiyama Kofun (Kofun, 1989)}} == Further Reading == {{small|Additional sources not used in this article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|title=Izumi City Excavation Report 26|publisher=Izumi City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun}} * [https://izbun.jp/introduction/shinoda/shinodakaibukiyamakofun.html Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun] - Cultural Property Section, Izumi City Board of Education [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Izumi, Osaka]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped Kofun]] [[category:new pages]][[ja:дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі]]

The mound's surface includes {{ill|fukiishi|ja|и‘әзҹі}} and {{ill|haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} (both cylindrical and figurative types), and it is surrounded by a moat. The kofun was designated as a historic site by Izumi City in 2003.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf List of Designated Cultural Properties in Izumi City]}}. == Cultural Properties == === Izumi City Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun - Designated on March 28, 2003. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes Help}} == Sources == {{small|Sources used in writing this article}} * Information plaque (Izumi City Board of Education, 2005) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Kaibukiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Encyclopedia of Japanese Historical Place Names|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»}} Osaka Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|е№іеҮЎзӨҫ}}|isbn=458249028X|ref=Kaibukiyama Kofun (Heibonsha, 1986)}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Takashi ЕҢtani|year=1989|chapter=Kaibukiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-dЕҚ Shuppan|ja|жқұдә¬е ӮеҮәзүҲ}}|isbn=4490102607|ref=Kaibukiyama Kofun (Kofun, 1989)}} == Further Reading == {{small|Additional sources not used in this article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|title=Izumi City Excavation Report 26|publisher=Izumi City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun}} * [https://izbun.jp/introduction/shinoda/shinodakaibukiyamakofun.html Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun] - Cultural Property Section, Izumi City Board of Education [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Izumi, Osaka]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped Kofun]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:pages to try to fix ills]] [[ja:дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі]]

{{Infobox ancient site |Name = Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|еӨ§йҳӘеәң}} {{ill|Izumi, Osaka|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮ}} TaichЕҚ |coordinates = {{coord|34|30|30.40|N|135|26|32.25|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun}} |Type = Scallop-shaped Kofun |Size = Mound length: 60m, height: 7.7m |Burial Facilities = Unknown |Artifacts = Steatite trough, {{ill|haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments |Accompanying Burial Mounds = None |Era = Early 5th century |Burial = Unknown |Historic Designation = Izumi City Designated Historic Site (Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun) |image = [[File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the mound and information plaque }} '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' (дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|еҸӨеўі}} located in {{ill|Izumi, Osaka|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮ}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|еӨ§йҳӘеәң}}. It is a scallop-shaped tumulus designated as a historic site by Izumi City. == Overview == {{thumb|right|250px|Panoramic view of the mound, with the rear circular part at the center and the short rectangular front to the left.}} The kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwest foothills of the Shinoda Hills in southern Osaka Prefecture. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the Edo-period uprisings (Chihara Riots), during which farmers reportedly used conch shells to signal and gather at the site.Information plaque at the site, installed by Izumi City Board of Education in 2005. Partial excavations have been conducted.Kaibukiyama Kofun, Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site (Tokyo-dЕҚ Shuppan, 1989). The mound has a scallop-shaped layout, with a short rectangular front extending northwestward. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular part having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound's surface includes {{ill|fukiishi|ja|и‘әзҹі}} and {{ill|haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} (both cylindrical and figurative types), and it is surrounded by a moat. The burial facility remains unidentified, but artifacts such as a steatite trough and haniwa fragments have been unearthed. The kofun is estimated to date back to the early 5th century during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј}}."Genealogy of Leaders in Northern Izumi," Izumi City's History Volume 6, pp. 65-70. In the northern Izumi region, the leader's burial mounds are divided into northern and southern clusters. A rotational leadership system is hypothesized based on temporal gaps between the clusters, with this kofun belonging to one such phase.Excavation Report on ЕҢzono Site (Izumi City Board of Education, 2014). The kofun was designated as a historic site by Izumi City in 2003.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf List of Designated Cultural Properties in Izumi City]}}. == Cultural Properties == === Izumi City Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun - Designated on March 28, 2003. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes Help}} == Sources == {{small|Sources used in writing this article}} * Information plaque (Izumi City Board of Education, 2005) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Kaibukiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Encyclopedia of Japanese Historical Place Names|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»}} Osaka Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|е№іеҮЎзӨҫ}}|isbn=458249028X|ref=Kaibukiyama Kofun (Heibonsha, 1986)}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Takashi ЕҢtani|year=1989|chapter=Kaibukiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-dЕҚ Shuppan|ja|жқұдә¬е ӮеҮәзүҲ}}|isbn=4490102607|ref=Kaibukiyama Kofun (Kofun, 1989)}} == Further Reading == {{small|Additional sources not used in this article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|title=Izumi City Excavation Report 26|publisher=Izumi City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun}} * [https://izbun.jp/introduction/shinoda/shinodakaibukiyamakofun.html Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun] - Cultural Property Section, Izumi City Board of Education [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Izumi, Osaka]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped Kofun]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Shita Kaibuki-yama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:pages to try to fix ills]] [[ja:дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі]]

The kofun is estimated to date back to the early 5th century during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј}}."Genealogy of Leaders in Northern Izumi," Izumi City's History Volume 6, pp. 65-70. In the northern Izumi region, the leader's burial mounds are divided into northern and southern clusters. A rotational leadership system is hypothesized based on temporal gaps between the clusters, with this kofun belonging to one such phase.Excavation Report on ЕҢzono Site (Izumi City Board of Education, 2014). The kofun was designated as a historic site by Izumi City in 2003.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf List of Designated Cultural Properties in Izumi City]}}. == Cultural Properties == === Izumi City Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun - Designated on March 28, 2003. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes Help}} == Sources == {{small|Sources used in writing this article}} * Information plaque (Izumi City Board of Education, 2005) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Kaibukiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Encyclopedia of Japanese Historical Place Names|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»}} Osaka Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|е№іеҮЎзӨҫ}}|isbn=458249028X|ref=Kaibukiyama Kofun (Heibonsha, 1986)}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Takashi ЕҢtani|year=1989|chapter=Kaibukiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-dЕҚ Shuppan|ja|жқұдә¬е ӮеҮәзүҲ}}|isbn=4490102607|ref=Kaibukiyama Kofun (Kofun, 1989)}} == Further Reading == {{small|Additional sources not used in this article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|title=Izumi City Excavation Report 26|publisher=Izumi City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun}} * [https://izbun.jp/introduction/shinoda/shinodakaibukiyamakofun.html Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun] - Cultural Property Section, Izumi City Board of Education [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Izumi, Osaka]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped Kofun]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Shita Kaibuki-yama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[ja:дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі]]

Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.Historical Site Information Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the form of a short-tailed {{ill|Hotategaigata Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|и‘әзҹі}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. Artifacts recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[5th century]], during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј}}. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was designated a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Izumi City official website). File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Full view of the mound

Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.Historical Site Information Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the form of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|и‘әзҹі}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. Artifacts recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[5th century]], during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј}}. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was designated a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Izumi City official website). File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Full view of the mound

Height: 7.7m | burial_chamber = Unknown | artifacts = Steatite trough, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments | period = Early [[5th century]] | designation = Izumi City Historic Site (2003) }} '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' (дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі}} located in {{ill|Izumi, Osaka|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮ}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|еӨ§йҳӘеәң}}, Japan. It has been designated a historic site by Izumi City. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ6}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65вҖ“70. ! colspan="2" | Group !! Tomb Name !! Shape !! Size !! Period |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|ja|е’Ңжіүй»„йҮ‘еЎҡеҸӨеўі}}|| {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|еҶҶеўі}} || 60вҖ“70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || ЕҢtsuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' || Hotategaigata Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|ЕҢzono Kofun|ja|еӨ§ең’еҸӨеўі}} || Hotategaigata Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Togidensha Kofun|ja|еҜҢжңЁи»ҠеЎҡеҸӨеўі}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.Historical Site Information Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the form of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|и‘әзҹі}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. Artifacts recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[5th century]], during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј}}. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was designated a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Izumi City official website). File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Full view of the mound

Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.Historical Site Information Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the form of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|и‘әзҹі}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. Artifacts recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[5th century]], during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј}}. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was designated a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Izumi City official website). File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Full view of the mound

Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.Historical Site Information Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the form of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|и‘әзҹі}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. Artifacts recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[5th century]], during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј}}. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was designated a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Izumi City official website). Period |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|ja|е’Ңжіүй»„йҮ‘еЎҡеҸӨеўі}}|| {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|еҶҶеўі}} || 60вҖ“70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || ЕҢtsuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' || Hotategaigata Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|ЕҢzono Kofun|ja|еӨ§ең’еҸӨеўі}} || Hotategaigata Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Togidensha Kofun|ja|еҜҢжңЁи»ҠеЎҡеҸӨеўі}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.Historical Site Information Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the form of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|и‘әзҹі}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. Artifacts recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[5th century]], during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј}}. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was designated a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Izumi City official website). File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Full view of the mound

Entrance to the mound and informational sign | alternate_name = | group = | location = {{ill|Izumi, Osaka|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮ}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|еӨ§йҳӘеәң}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|30.40|N|135|26|32.25|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Hotategaigata Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі}} | size = Total mound length: 60m
Height: 7.7m | burial_chamber = Unknown | artifacts = Steatite trough, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments | period = Early [[5th century]] | designation = Izumi City Historic Site (2003) }} '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' (дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі}} located in {{ill|Izumi, Osaka|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮ}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|еӨ§йҳӘеәң}}, Japan. It has been designated a historic site by Izumi City. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ6}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65вҖ“70. ! colspan="2" | Group !! Tomb name !! Shape !! Size !! Period |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|ja|е’Ңжіүй»„йҮ‘еЎҡеҸӨеўі}}|| {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|еҶҶеўі}} || 60вҖ“70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || ЕҢtsuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' || Hotategaigata Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|ЕҢzono Kofun|ja|еӨ§ең’еҸӨеўі}} || Hotategaigata Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Togidensha Kofun|ja|еҜҢжңЁи»ҠеЎҡеҸӨеўі}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.Historical Site Information Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the form of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|и‘әзҹі}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. Artifacts recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[5th century]], during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј}}. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was designated a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Izumi City official website). File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Full view of the mound
{{small|Center: rear circular section, left: front section.}} == Cultural Properties == === Izumi City Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun вҖ“ Designated on March 28, 2003. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for this article)}} * Historical Site Information Board (Izumi City Education Board, 2005) * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=ЕҢtani Takeshi|year=1989|chapter=Kaibukiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Kaibukiyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|year=1986|chapter=Kaibukiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Topography Series|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»}} Osaka Volume|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Kaibukiyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun}} * [https://izbun.jp/introduction/shinoda/shinodakaibukiyamakofun.html Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun - Cultural Properties of Izumi City] (Izumi City Education Board) [[Category:dd]][[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:History of Izumi, Osaka]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Hotategaigata Kofun]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun]][[Category:dd]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]] [[ja:дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі]]

Height: 7.7m | burial_chamber = Unknown | artifacts = Steatite trough, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments | period = Early [[5th century]] | designation = Izumi City Historic Site (2003) }} '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' (дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі}} located in {{ill|Izumi, Osaka|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮ}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|еӨ§йҳӘеәң}}, Japan. It has been designated a historic site by Izumi City. == Overview ==

Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.Historical Site Information Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the form of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|и‘әзҹі}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. Artifacts recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[5th century]], during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј}}. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was designated a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Izumi City official website). File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Full view of the mound

Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.Historical Site Information Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the form of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|и‘әзҹі}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. Artifacts recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[5th century]], during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј}}. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was designated a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Izumi City official website). File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Full view of the mound

Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the form of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|и‘әзҹі}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. Artifacts recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[5th century]], during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј}}. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was designated a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Izumi City official website). Height: 7.7m | burial_chamber = Unknown | artifacts = Steatite trough, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments | period = Early [[5th century]] | designation = Izumi City Historic Site (2003) }} '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' (дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі}} located in {{ill|Izumi, Osaka|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮ}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|еӨ§йҳӘеәң}}, Japan. It has been designated a historic site by Izumi City. == Overview ==

Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.Historical Site Information Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the form of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|и‘әзҹі}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. Artifacts recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[5th century]], during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј}}. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was designated a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Izumi City official website). {{Infobox ancient site | name = Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun (дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі) | image = [[File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the mound and informational sign | alternate_name = | group = | location = {{ill|Izumi, Osaka|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮ}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|еӨ§йҳӘеәң}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|30.40|N|135|26|32.25|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Hotategaigata Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі}} | size = Total mound length: 60m
Height: 7.7m | burial_chamber = Unknown | artifacts = Steatite trough, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments | period = Early [[5th century]] | designation = Izumi City Historic Site (2003) }} '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' (дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі}} located in {{ill|Izumi, Osaka|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮ}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|еӨ§йҳӘеәң}}, Japan. It has been designated a historic site by Izumi City. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ6}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65вҖ“70. ! colspan="2" | Group !! Tomb name !! Shape !! Size !! Period |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|ja|е’Ңжіүй»„йҮ‘еЎҡеҸӨеўі}}|| {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|еҶҶеўі}} || 60вҖ“70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || ЕҢtsuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' || Hotategaigata Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|ЕҢzono Kofun|ja|еӨ§ең’еҸӨеўі}} || Hotategaigata Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Togidensha Kofun|ja|еҜҢжңЁи»ҠеЎҡеҸӨеўі}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.Historical Site Information Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the form of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|и‘әзҹі}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. Artifacts recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[5th century]], during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј}}. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was designated a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Izumi City official website). File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Full view of the mound

Height: 7.7m | burial_chamber = Unknown | artifacts = Steatite trough, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments | period = Early [[5th century]] | designation = Izumi City Historic Site (2003) }} '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' (дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі}} located in {{ill|Izumi, Osaka|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮ}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|еӨ§йҳӘеәң}}, Japan. It has been designated a historic site by Izumi City. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ6}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65вҖ“70. ! colspan="2" | Group !! Tomb name !! Shape !! Size !! Period |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|ja|е’Ңжіүй»„йҮ‘еЎҡеҸӨеўі}}|| {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|еҶҶеўі}} || 60вҖ“70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || ЕҢtsuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' || Hotategaigata Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|ЕҢzono Kofun|ja|еӨ§ең’еҸӨеўі}} || Hotategaigata Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Togidensha Kofun|ja|еҜҢжңЁи»ҠеЎҡеҸӨеўі}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.Historical Site Information Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the form of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|и‘әзҹі}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. Artifacts recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[5th century]], during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј}}. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was designated a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Izumi City official website). File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Full view of the mound

The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.Historical Site Information Board, Izumi City, 2005. Artifacts recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[5th century]], during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј}}. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. {{Infobox ancient site | name = Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun (дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі) | image = [[File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the mound and informational sign | alternate_name = | group = | location = {{ill|Izumi, Osaka|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮ}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|еӨ§йҳӘеәң}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|30.40|N|135|26|32.25|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Hotategaigata Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі}} | size = Total mound length: 60m
Height: 7.7m | burial_chamber = Unknown | artifacts = Steatite trough, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments | period = Early [[5th century]] | designation = Izumi City Historic Site (2003) }} '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' (дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі}} located in {{ill|Izumi, Osaka|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮ}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|еӨ§йҳӘеәң}}, Japan. It has been designated a historic site by Izumi City. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ6}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65вҖ“70. ! colspan="2" | Group !! Tomb name !! Shape !! Size !! Period |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|ja|е’Ңжіүй»„йҮ‘еЎҡеҸӨеўі}}|| {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|еҶҶеўі}} || 60вҖ“70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || ЕҢtsuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' || Hotategaigata Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|ЕҢzono Kofun|ja|еӨ§ең’еҸӨеўі}} || Hotategaigata Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Togidensha Kofun|ja|еҜҢжңЁи»ҠеЎҡеҸӨеўі}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.Historical Site Information Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the form of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|и‘әзҹі}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. Artifacts recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[5th century]], during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј}}. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was designated a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Izumi City official website). File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Full view of the mound

Height: 7.7m | burial_chamber = Unknown | artifacts = Steatite trough, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments | period = Early [[5th century]] | designation = Izumi City Historic Site (2003) }} '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' (дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі}} located in {{ill|Izumi, Osaka|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮ}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|еӨ§йҳӘеәң}}, Japan. It has been designated a historic site by Izumi City. == Overview ==

Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.Historical Site Information Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the form of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|и‘әзҹі}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. Artifacts recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[5th century]], during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј}}. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was designated a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Izumi City official website). File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Full view of the mound

It has been designated a historic site by Izumi City. == Overview ==

Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.Historical Site Information Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the form of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|и‘әзҹі}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. Artifacts recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[5th century]], during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј}}. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was designated a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Izumi City official website).
