Height: 7.7m | burial_chamber = Unknown | artifacts = Steatite trough, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments | period = Early [[5th century]] | designation = Izumi City Historic Site (2003) }} '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' (дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in {{ill|Izumi, Osaka|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮ}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|еӨ§йҳӘеәң}}, Japan. It has been designated a historic site by Izumi City. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ6}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65вҖ“70. ! colspan="2" | part_of !! Tomb name !! type !! area !! Period |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|ja|е’Ңжіүй»„йҮ‘еЎҡеҸӨеўі}}|| {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|еҶҶеўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} || 60вҖ“70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || ЕҢtsuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' || Hotategaigata Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|ЕҢzono Kofun|ja|еӨ§ең’еҸӨеўі}} || Hotategaigata Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Togidensha Kofun|ja|еҜҢжңЁи»ҠеЎҡеҸӨеўі}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.other_designation Information Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the form of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|и‘әзҹі}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. Artifacts recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[5th century]], during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј}}. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was designated a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Izumi City official website). File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Full view of the mound

Entrance to the mound and informational sign | alternate_name = | part_of = | location = {{ill|Izumi, Osaka|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮ}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|еӨ§йҳӘеәң}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|30.40|N|135|26|32.25|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Hotategaigata Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun}} | size = Total mound length: 60m
Height: 7.7m | burial_chamber = Unknown | artifacts = Steatite trough, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments | period = Early [[5th century]] | designation = Izumi City Historic Site (2003) }} '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' (дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in {{ill|Izumi, Osaka|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮ}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|еӨ§йҳӘеәң}}, Japan. It has been designated a historic site by Izumi City. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ6}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65вҖ“70. ! colspan="2" | part_of !! Tomb name !! type !! area !! Period |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|ja|е’Ңжіүй»„йҮ‘еЎҡеҸӨеўі}}|| {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|еҶҶеўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} || 60вҖ“70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || ЕҢtsuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' || Hotategaigata Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|ЕҢzono Kofun|ja|еӨ§ең’еҸӨеўі}} || Hotategaigata Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Togidensha Kofun|ja|еҜҢжңЁи»ҠеЎҡеҸӨеўі}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.other_designation Information Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the form of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|и‘әзҹі}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. Artifacts recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[5th century]], during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј}}. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was designated a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Izumi City official website). File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Full view of the mound

Entrance to the mound and informational sign | alternate_name = | part_of = | location = {{ill|Izumi, Osaka|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮ}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|еӨ§йҳӘеәң}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|30.40|N|135|26|32.25|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Hotategaigata Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun}} | size = Total mound length: 60m
Height: 7.7m | burial_chamber = Unknown | artifacts = Steatite trough, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments | period = Early [[5th century]] | designation = Izumi City Historic Site (2003) }} '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' (дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in {{ill|Izumi, Osaka|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮ}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|еӨ§йҳӘеәң}}, Japan. It has been designated a historic site by Izumi City. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ6}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65вҖ“70. ! colspan="2" | part_of !! Tomb name !! type !! area !! Period |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|ja|е’Ңжіүй»„йҮ‘еЎҡеҸӨеўі}}|| {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|еҶҶеўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} || 60вҖ“70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || ЕҢtsuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' || Hotategaigata Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|ЕҢzono Kofun|ja|еӨ§ең’еҸӨеўі}} || Hotategaigata Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Togidensha Kofun|ja|еҜҢжңЁи»ҠеЎҡеҸӨеўі}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.other_designation Information Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the form of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|и‘әзҹі}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. Artifacts recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[5th century]], during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј}}. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was designated a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated other_designation]}} (Izumi City official website). File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Full view of the mound

Height: 7.7m | burial_chamber = Unknown | artifacts = Steatite trough, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments | period = Early [[5th century]] | designation = Izumi City Historic Site (2003) }} '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' (дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in {{ill|Izumi, Osaka|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮ}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|еӨ§йҳӘеәң}}, Japan. It has been designated a historic site by Izumi City. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ6}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65вҖ“70. ! colspan="2" | part_of !! Tomb name !! type !! area !! Period |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|ja|е’Ңжіүй»„йҮ‘еЎҡеҸӨеўі}}|| {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|еҶҶеўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} || 60вҖ“70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || ЕҢtsuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' || Hotategaigata Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|ЕҢzono Kofun|ja|еӨ§ең’еҸӨеўі}} || Hotategaigata Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Togidensha Kofun|ja|еҜҢжңЁи»ҠеЎҡеҸӨеўі}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.other_designation Information Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the form of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|и‘әзҹі}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. Artifacts recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[5th century]], during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј}}. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was designated a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated other_designation]}} (Izumi City official website). Height: 7.7m | burial_chamber = Unknown | artifacts = Steatite trough, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments | period = Early [[5th century]] | designation = Izumi City Historic Site (2003) }} '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' (дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in {{ill|Izumi, Osaka|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮ}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|еӨ§йҳӘеәң}}, Japan. It has been designated a historic site by Izumi City. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ6}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65вҖ“70. ! colspan="2" | part_of !! Tomb name !! type !! area !! Period |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|ja|е’Ңжіүй»„йҮ‘еЎҡеҸӨеўі}}|| {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|еҶҶеўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} || 60вҖ“70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || ЕҢtsuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' || Hotategaigata Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|ЕҢzono Kofun|ja|еӨ§ең’еҸӨеўі}} || Hotategaigata Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Togidensha Kofun|ja|еҜҢжңЁи»ҠеЎҡеҸӨеўі}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.other_designation Information Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the form of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|и‘әзҹі}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. Artifacts recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[5th century]], during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј}}. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was designated a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated other_designation]}} (Izumi City official website). Height: 7.7m | burial_chamber = Unknown | artifacts = Steatite trough, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments | period = Early [[5th century]] | designation = Izumi City other_designation (2003) }} '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' (дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in {{ill|Izumi, Osaka|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮ}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|еӨ§йҳӘеәң}}, Japan. It has been designated a historic site by Izumi City. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ6}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65вҖ“70. ! colspan="2" | part_of !! Tomb name !! type !! area !! Period |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|ja|е’Ңжіүй»„йҮ‘еЎҡеҸӨеўі}}|| {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|еҶҶеўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} || 60вҖ“70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || ЕҢtsuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' || Hotategaigata Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|ЕҢzono Kofun|ja|еӨ§ең’еҸӨеўі}} || Hotategaigata Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Togidensha Kofun|ja|еҜҢжңЁи»ҠеЎҡеҸӨеўі}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.other_designation Information Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the form of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|и‘әзҹі}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. Artifacts recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[5th century]], during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј}}. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was designated a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated other_designation]}} (Izumi City official website). Height: 7.7m | burial_chamber = Unknown | artifacts = Steatite trough, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments | period = Early [[5th century]] | designation = Izumi City other_designation (2003) }} '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' (дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in {{ill|Izumi, Osaka|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮ}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|еӨ§йҳӘеәң}}, Japan. It has been designated a historic site by Izumi City. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ6}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65вҖ“70. ! colspan="2" | part_of !! Tomb name !! type !! area !! Period |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|ja|е’Ңжіүй»„йҮ‘еЎҡеҸӨеўі}}|| {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|еҶҶеўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} || 60вҖ“70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || ЕҢtsuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' || Hotategaigata Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|ЕҢzono Kofun|ja|еӨ§ең’еҸӨеўі}} || Hotategaigata Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Togidensha Kofun|ja|еҜҢжңЁи»ҠеЎҡеҸӨеўі}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.other_designation Information Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the form of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|и‘әзҹі}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[5th century]], during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј}}. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was designated a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated other_designation]}} (Izumi City official website). Entrance to the mound and intypeational sign | alternate_name = | part_of = | location = {{ill|Izumi, Osaka|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮ}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|еӨ§йҳӘеәң}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|30.40|N|135|26|32.25|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Hotategaigata Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun}} | size = Total mound length: 60m
Height: 7.7m | burial_chamber = Unknown | burial goods = Steatite trough, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments | period = Early [[5th century]] | other_designation = Izumi City other_other_designation (2003) }} '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' (дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in {{ill|Izumi, Osaka|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮ}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|еӨ§йҳӘеәң}}, Japan. It has been other_designation1 a historic site by Izumi City. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ6}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65вҖ“70. ! colspan="2" | part_of !! Tomb name !! type !! area !! Period |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|ja|е’Ңжіүй»„йҮ‘еЎҡеҸӨеўі}}|| {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|еҶҶеўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} || 60вҖ“70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || ЕҢtsuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' || Hotategaigata Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|ЕҢzono Kofun|ja|еӨ§ең’еҸӨеўі}} || Hotategaigata Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Togidensha Kofun|ja|еҜҢжңЁи»ҠеЎҡеҸӨеўі}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.other_other_designation Intypeation Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the type of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|и‘әзҹі}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[5th century]], during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј}}. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was other_designation1 a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated other_other_designation]}} (Izumi City official website). File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Full view of the mound

It has been other_designation1 a historic site by Izumi City. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ6}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65вҖ“70. ! colspan="2" | part_of !! Tomb name !! type !! area !! Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.other_other_designation Intypeation Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the type of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|и‘әзҹі}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[5th century]], during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј}}. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was other_designation1 a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated other_other_designation]}} (Izumi City official website). Height: 7.7m | burial_chamber = Unknown | burial goods = Steatite trough, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments | period = Early [[5th century]] | other_designation = Izumi City other_designation (2003) }} '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' (дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in {{ill|Izumi, Osaka|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮ}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|еӨ§йҳӘеәң}}, Japan. It has been other_designation1 a historic site by Izumi City. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ6}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65вҖ“70. ! colspan="2" | part_of !! Tomb name !! type !! area !! Period |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|ja|е’Ңжіүй»„йҮ‘еЎҡеҸӨеўі}}|| {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|еҶҶеўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} || 60вҖ“70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || ЕҢtsuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' || Hotategaigata Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|ЕҢzono Kofun|ja|еӨ§ең’еҸӨеўі}} || Hotategaigata Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Togidensha Kofun|ja|еҜҢжңЁи»ҠеЎҡеҸӨеўі}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.other_designation Intypeation Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the type of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|и‘әзҹі}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[5th century]], during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј}}. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was other_designation1 a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated other_designation]}} (Izumi City official website). File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Full view of the mound
{{small|Center: rear circular section, left: front section.}} == other_designation == === Izumi City Designated other_designation === * other_designation ** Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun вҖ“ Designated on The mound is in the type of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun}} with the short tail facing northwest. Height: 7.7m | burial = Unknown | burial goods = Steatite trough, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments | period = Early [[5th century]] | other_designation = Izumi City other_designation (2003) }} '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' (дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in {{ill|Izumi, Osaka|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮ}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|еӨ§йҳӘеәң}}, Japan. It has been other_designation1 a historic site by Izumi City. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ6}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65вҖ“70. ! colspan="2" | part_of !! Tomb name !! type !! area !! Period |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|ja|е’Ңжіүй»„йҮ‘еЎҡеҸӨеўі}}|| {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|еҶҶеўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} || 60вҖ“70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || ЕҢtsuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' || Hotategaigata Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|ЕҢzono Kofun|ja|еӨ§ең’еҸӨеўі}} || Hotategaigata Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Togidensha Kofun|ja|еҜҢжңЁи»ҠеЎҡеҸӨеўі}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.other_designation Intypeation Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the type of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|и‘әзҹі}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[5th century]], during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј}}. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was other_designation1 a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated other_designation]}} (Izumi City official website). File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Full view of the mound

Height: 7.7m | burial = Unknown | burial goods = Steatite trough, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments | period = Early [[5th century]] | other_designation = Izumi City other_designation (2003) }} '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' (дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in {{ill|Izumi, Osaka|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮ}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|еӨ§йҳӘеәң}}, Japan. It has been other_designation1 a historic site by Izumi City. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ6}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65вҖ“70. ! colspan="2" | part_of !! Tomb name !! type !! area !! Period |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|ja|е’Ңжіүй»„йҮ‘еЎҡеҸӨеўі}}|| {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|еҶҶеўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} || 60вҖ“70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || ЕҢtsuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' || Hotategaigata Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|ЕҢzono Kofun|ja|еӨ§ең’еҸӨеўі}} || Hotategaigata Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Togidensha Kofun|ja|еҜҢжңЁи»ҠеЎҡеҸӨеўі}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.other_designation Intypeation Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the type of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|и‘әзҹі}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[5th century]], during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј}}. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was other_designation1 a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated other_designation]}} (Izumi City official website). File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Full view of the mound

{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ6}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65вҖ“70. ! colspan="2" | part_of !! Tomb name !! type !! area !! Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.other_designation Intypeation Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the type of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|и‘әзҹі}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[5th century]], during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј}}. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was other_designation1 a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated other_designation]}} (Izumi City official website). It has been other_designation1 a historic site by Izumi City. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ6}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65вҖ“70. ! colspan="2" | part_of !! Tomb name !! type !! area !! Period |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|ja|е’Ңжіүй»„йҮ‘еЎҡеҸӨеўі}}|| {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|еҶҶеўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} || 60вҖ“70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || ЕҢtsuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' || Hotategaigata Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|ЕҢzono Kofun|ja|еӨ§ең’еҸӨеўі}} || Hotategaigata Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Togidensha Kofun|ja|еҜҢжңЁи»ҠеЎҡеҸӨеўі}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.other_designation Intypeation Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the type of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|и‘әзҹі}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[5th century]], during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј}}. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was other_designation1 a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated other_designation]}} (Izumi City official website). revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі}} | name = Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun (дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі) | image = [[File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the mound and intypeational sign | alternate_name = | part_of = | location = {{ill|Izumi, Osaka|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮ}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|еӨ§йҳӘеәң}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|30.40|N|135|26|32.25|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Hotategaigata Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun}} | area = Total mound length: 60m
Height: 7.7m | burial = Unknown | burial goods = Steatite trough, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments | period = Early [[5th century]] | other_designation = Izumi City other_designation (2003) }} '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' (дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in {{ill|Izumi, Osaka|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮ}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|еӨ§йҳӘеәң}}, Japan. It has been other_designation1 a historic site by Izumi City. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ6}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65вҖ“70. ! colspan="2" | part_of !! Tomb name !! type !! area !! Period |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|ja|е’Ңжіүй»„йҮ‘еЎҡеҸӨеўі}}|| {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|еҶҶеўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} || 60вҖ“70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || ЕҢtsuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' || Hotategaigata Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|ЕҢzono Kofun|ja|еӨ§ең’еҸӨеўі}} || Hotategaigata Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Togidensha Kofun|ja|еҜҢжңЁи»ҠеЎҡеҸӨеўі}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.other_designation Intypeation Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the type of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|и‘әзҹі}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[5th century]], during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј}}. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was other_designation1 a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated other_designation]}} (Izumi City official website). File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Full view of the mound

The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|и‘әзҹі}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[5th century]], during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј}}. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was other_designation1 a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated other_designation]}} (Izumi City official website). Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.other_designation Intypeation Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the type of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|и‘әзҹі}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[5th century]], during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј}}. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was other_designation1 a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated other_designation]}} (Izumi City official website). The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.other_designation Intypeation Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the type of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|и‘әзҹі}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[5th century]], during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј}}. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was other_designation1 a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated other_designation]}} (Izumi City official website). File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Full view of the mound

Height: 7.7m | burial = Unknown | burial goods = Steatite trough, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments | period = Early [[5th century]] | other_designation = Izumi City other_designation (2003) }} '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' (дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in {{ill|Izumi, Osaka|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮ}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|еӨ§йҳӘеәң}}, Japan. It has been other_designation1 a historic site by Izumi City. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ6}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65вҖ“70. ! colspan="2" | part_of !! Tomb name !! type !! area !! built |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|ja|е’Ңжіүй»„йҮ‘еЎҡеҸӨеўі}}|| {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|еҶҶеўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} || 60вҖ“70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || ЕҢtsuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' || Hotategaigata Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|ЕҢzono Kofun|ja|еӨ§ең’еҸӨеўі}} || Hotategaigata Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Togidensha Kofun|ja|еҜҢжңЁи»ҠеЎҡеҸӨеўі}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.other_designation Intypeation Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the type of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|и‘әзҹі}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[5th century]], during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј}}. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was other_designation1 a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated other_designation]}} (Izumi City official website). File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Full view of the mound

Tomb name !! type !! area !! built |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|ja|е’Ңжіүй»„йҮ‘еЎҡеҸӨеўі}}|| {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|еҶҶеўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} || 60вҖ“70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || ЕҢtsuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' || Hotategaigata Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|ЕҢzono Kofun|ja|еӨ§ең’еҸӨеўі}} || Hotategaigata Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Togidensha Kofun|ja|еҜҢжңЁи»ҠеЎҡеҸӨеўі}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.other_designation Intypeation Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the type of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|и‘әзҹі}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[5th century]], during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј}}. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was other_designation1 a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated other_designation]}} (Izumi City official website). Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.Historic Site Intypeation Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the type of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|и‘әзҹі}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[5th century]], during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј}}. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Historic Site]}} (Izumi City official website). Height: 7.7m | burial = Unknown | burial goods = Steatite trough, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments | period = Early [[5th century]] | Historic Site = Izumi City Historic Site (2003) }} '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' (дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in {{ill|Izumi, Osaka|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮ}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|еӨ§йҳӘеәң}}, Japan. It has been Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ6}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65вҖ“70. ! colspan="2" | part_of !! Tomb name !! type !! area !! built |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|ja|е’Ңжіүй»„йҮ‘еЎҡеҸӨеўі}}|| {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|еҶҶеўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} || 60вҖ“70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || ЕҢtsuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' || Hotategaigata Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|ЕҢzono Kofun|ja|еӨ§ең’еҸӨеўі}} || Hotategaigata Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Togidensha Kofun|ja|еҜҢжңЁи»ҠеЎҡеҸӨеўі}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.Historic Site Intypeation Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the type of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|и‘әзҹі}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[5th century]], during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј}}. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Historic Site]}} (Izumi City official website). File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Full view of the mound

Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.Historic Site Intypeation Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the type of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|и‘әзҹі}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[5th century]], during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј}}. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Historic Site]}} (Izumi City official website). Height: 7.7m | burial = Unknown | burial goods = Steatite trough, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments | period = Early [[5th century]] | Historic Site = Izumi City Historic Site (2003) }} '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' (дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in {{ill|Izumi, Osaka|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮ}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|еӨ§йҳӘеәң}}, Japan. It has been Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ6}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65вҖ“70. ! colspan="2" | part_of !! Tomb name !! type !! area !! built |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|ja|е’Ңжіүй»„йҮ‘еЎҡеҸӨеўі}}|| {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|еҶҶеўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} || 60вҖ“70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || ЕҢtsuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' || Hotategaigata Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|ЕҢzono Kofun|ja|еӨ§ең’еҸӨеўі}} || Hotategaigata Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Togidensha Kofun|ja|еҜҢжңЁи»ҠеЎҡеҸӨеўі}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.Historic Site Intypeation Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the type of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|и‘әзҹі}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[5th century]], during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј}}. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Historic Site]}} (Izumi City official website). Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.Historic Site Intypeation Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the type of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|и‘әзҹі}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[5th century]], during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј}}. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Historic Site]}} (Izumi City official website).
