{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі}} | name = Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun (дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі) | image = [[File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the mound and intypeational sign | alternate_name = | part_of = | location = {{ill|Izumi, Osaka|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮ}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|еӨ§йҳӘеәң}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|30.40|N|135|26|32.25|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Hotategaigata Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun}} | area = Total mound length: 60m
Height: 7.7m | burial = Unknown | burial goods = Steatite trough, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments | period = Early [[5th century]] | Historic Site = Izumi City Historic Site (2003) }} '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' (дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in {{ill|Izumi, Osaka|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮ}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|еӨ§йҳӘеәң}}, Japan. It has been Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ6}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65вҖ“70. ! colspan="2" | part_of !! Tomb name !! type !! area !! built |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|ja|е’Ңжіүй»„йҮ‘еЎҡеҸӨеўі}}|| {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|еҶҶеўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} || 60вҖ“70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || ЕҢtsuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' || Hotategaigata Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|ЕҢzono Kofun|ja|еӨ§ең’еҸӨеўі}} || Hotategaigata Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Togidensha Kofun|ja|еҜҢжңЁи»ҠеЎҡеҸӨеўі}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.Historic Site Intypeation Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the type of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|и‘әзҹі}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[5th century]], during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј}}. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Historic Site]}} (Izumi City official website). File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Full view of the mound
{{small|Center: rear circular section, left: front section.}} == Historic Site == === Izumi City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun вҖ“ Designated on March 28, 2003. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for this article)}} * Historic Site Intypeation Board (Izumi City Education Board, 2005) * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=ЕҢtani Takeshi|year=1989|chapter=Kaibukiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Kaibukiyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|year=1986|chapter=Kaibukiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Topography Series|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»}} Osaka Volume|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Kaibukiyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun}} * [https://izbun.jp/introduction/shinoda/shinodakaibukiyamakofun.html Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun - Historic Site of Izumi City] (Izumi City Education Board) {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Izumi, Osaka]] [[Category:Hotategaigata Kofun]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun]] [[ja:дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі]]

Tomb name !! type !! area !! built |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|ja|е’Ңжіүй»„йҮ‘еЎҡеҸӨеўі|12=draft}}|| {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|еҶҶеўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} || 60вҖ“70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || ЕҢtsuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' || Hotategaigata Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|ЕҢzono Kofun|ja|еӨ§ең’еҸӨеўі|12=draft}} || Hotategaigata Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Togidensha Kofun|ja|еҜҢжңЁи»ҠеЎҡеҸӨеўі|12=draft}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө|12=draft}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј|12=draft}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.Historic Site Intypeation Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=draft}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the type of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|и‘әзҹі|12=draft}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ|12=draft}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[5th century]], during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј|12=draft}}. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Historic Site]}} (Izumi City official website). File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Full view of the mound
{{small|Center: rear circular section, left: front section.}} == Historic Site == === Izumi City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun вҖ“ Designated on March 28, 2003. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for this article)}} * Historic Site Intypeation Board (Izumi City Education Board, 2005) * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=ЕҢtani Takeshi|year=1989|chapter=Kaibukiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=draft}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Kaibukiyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|year=1986|chapter=Kaibukiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Topography Series|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»|12=draft}} Osaka Volume|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Kaibukiyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun}} * [https://izbun.jp/introduction/shinoda/shinodakaibukiyamakofun.html Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun - Historic Site of Izumi City] (Izumi City Education Board) [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Izumi, Osaka]] [[Category:Hotategaigata Kofun]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun]] [[ja:дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі]]

Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө|12=draft}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј|12=draft}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.Historic Site Intypeation Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=draft}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the type of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|и‘әзҹі|12=draft}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ|12=draft}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[5th century]], during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј|12=draft}}. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Historic Site]}} (Izumi City official website). File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Full view of the mound

Tomb name !! type !! area !! built |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|ja|е’Ңжіүй»„йҮ‘еЎҡеҸӨеўі|12=draft}}|| {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|еҶҶеўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} || 60вҖ“70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || ЕҢtsuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' || Hotategaigata Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|ЕҢzono Kofun|ja|еӨ§ең’еҸӨеўі|12=draft}} || Hotategaigata Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Togidensha Kofun|ja|еҜҢжңЁи»ҠеЎҡеҸӨеўі|12=draft}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө|12=draft}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј|12=draft}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.Historic Site Intypeation Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=draft}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the type of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|и‘әзҹі|12=draft}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ|12=draft}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[5th century]], during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј|12=draft}}. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Historic Site]}} (Izumi City official website). File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Full view of the mound

Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|12=draft}} | name = Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun (дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі) | image = [[File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the mound and intypeational sign | alternate_name = | part_of = | location = {{ill|Izumi, Osaka|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮ|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|еӨ§йҳӘеәң|12=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|30.40|N|135|26|32.25|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Hotategaigata Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}} | area = Total mound length: 60m
Height: 7.7m | burial = Unknown | burial goods = Steatite trough, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ|12=draft}} fragments | period = Early [[5th century]] | Historic Site = Izumi City Historic Site (2003) }} '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' (дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Izumi, Osaka|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮ|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|еӨ§йҳӘеәң|12=draft}}, Japan. It has been Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ6|12=draft}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65вҖ“70. ! colspan="2" | part_of !! Tomb name !! type !! area !! built |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|ja|е’Ңжіүй»„йҮ‘еЎҡеҸӨеўі|12=draft}}|| {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|еҶҶеўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} || 60вҖ“70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || ЕҢtsuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' || Hotategaigata Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|ЕҢzono Kofun|ja|еӨ§ең’еҸӨеўі|12=draft}} || Hotategaigata Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Togidensha Kofun|ja|еҜҢжңЁи»ҠеЎҡеҸӨеўі|12=draft}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө|12=draft}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј|12=draft}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.Historic Site Intypeation Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=draft}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the type of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|и‘әзҹі|12=draft}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ|12=draft}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[5th century]], during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј|12=draft}}. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Historic Site]}} (Izumi City official website). File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Full view of the mound

Tomb name !! type !! area !! built |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|ja|е’Ңжіүй»„йҮ‘еЎҡеҸӨеўі|12=draft}}|| {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|еҶҶеўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} || 60вҖ“70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || ЕҢtsuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' || Hotategaigata Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|ЕҢzono Kofun|ja|еӨ§ең’еҸӨеўі|12=draft}} || Hotategaigata Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Togidensha Kofun|ja|еҜҢжңЁи»ҠеЎҡеҸӨеўі|12=draft}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө|12=draft}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј|12=draft}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.Historic Site Intypeation Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=draft}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the type of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|и‘әзҹі|12=draft}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ|12=draft}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[5th century]], during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј|12=draft}}. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Historic Site]}} (Izumi City official website). File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Full view of the mound
{{small|Center: rear circular section, left: front section.}} == Historic Site == === Izumi City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun вҖ“ Designated on March 28, 2003. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for this article)}} * Historic Site Intypeation Board (Izumi City Education Board, 2005) * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=ЕҢtani Takeshi|year=1989|chapter=Kaibukiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=draft}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Kaibukiyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|year=1986|chapter=Kaibukiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Topography Series|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»|12=draft}} Osaka Volume|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Kaibukiyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun}} * [https://izbun.jp/introduction/shinoda/shinodakaibukiyamakofun.html Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun - Historic Site of Izumi City] (Izumi City Education Board) [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Izumi, Osaka]] [[Category:Hotategaigata Kofun]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun]] [[ja:дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}}

Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|12=draft}} | name = Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun (дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі) | image = [[File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the mound and intypeational sign | alternate_name = | part_of = | location = {{ill|Izumi, Osaka|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮ|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|еӨ§йҳӘеәң|12=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|30.40|N|135|26|32.25|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Hotategaigata Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}} | area = Total mound length: 60m
Height: 7.7m | burial = Unknown | burial goods = Steatite trough, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ|12=draft}} fragments | period = Early [[5th century]] | Historic Site = Izumi City Historic Site (2003) }} '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' (дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Izumi, Osaka|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮ|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|еӨ§йҳӘеәң|12=draft}}, Japan. It has been Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ6|12=draft}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65вҖ“70. ! colspan="2" | part_of !! Tomb name !! type !! area !! built |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|ja|е’Ңжіүй»„йҮ‘еЎҡеҸӨеўі|12=draft}}|| {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|еҶҶеўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} || 60вҖ“70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || ЕҢtsuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' || Hotategaigata Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|ЕҢzono Kofun|ja|еӨ§ең’еҸӨеўі|12=draft}} || Hotategaigata Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Togidensha Kofun|ja|еҜҢжңЁи»ҠеЎҡеҸӨеўі|12=draft}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө|12=draft}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј|12=draft}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.Historic Site Intypeation Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=draft}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the type of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|и‘әзҹі|12=draft}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ|12=draft}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[5th century]], during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј|12=draft}}. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Historic Site]}} (Izumi City official website). File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Full view of the mound

Tomb name !! type !! area !! built |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|ja|е’Ңжіүй»„йҮ‘еЎҡеҸӨеўі|12=draft|12=draft}}|| {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|еҶҶеўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} || 60вҖ“70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || ЕҢtsuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' || Hotategaigata Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|ЕҢzono Kofun|ja|еӨ§ең’еҸӨеўі|12=draft|12=draft}} || Hotategaigata Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Togidensha Kofun|ja|еҜҢжңЁи»ҠеЎҡеҸӨеўі|12=draft|12=draft}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө|12=draft|12=draft}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј|12=draft|12=draft}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.Historic Site Intypeation Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the type of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|и‘әзҹі|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ|12=draft|12=draft}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[5th century]], during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј|12=draft|12=draft}}. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Historic Site]}} (Izumi City official website). File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Full view of the mound
{{small|Center: rear circular section, left: front section.}} == Historic Site == === Izumi City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun вҖ“ Designated on March 28, 2003. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for this article)}} * Historic Site Intypeation Board (Izumi City Education Board, 2005) * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=ЕҢtani Takeshi|year=1989|chapter=Kaibukiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Kaibukiyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|year=1986|chapter=Kaibukiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Topography Series|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»|12=draft|12=draft}} Osaka Volume|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Kaibukiyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun}} * [https://izbun.jp/introduction/shinoda/shinodakaibukiyamakofun.html Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun - Historic Site of Izumi City] (Izumi City Education Board) {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Izumi, Osaka]] [[Category:Hotategaigata Kofun]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun]] [[ja:дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі]]

Height: 7.7m | burial = Unknown | burial goods = Steatite trough, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ|12=draft|12=draft}} fragments | period = Early [[5th century]] | Historic Site = Izumi City Historic Site (2003) }} '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' (дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Izumi, Osaka|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮ|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|еӨ§йҳӘеәң|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan. It has been Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ6|12=draft|12=draft}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65вҖ“70. ! colspan="2" | part_of !! Tomb name !! type !! area !! built |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|ja|е’Ңжіүй»„йҮ‘еЎҡеҸӨеўі|12=draft|12=draft}}|| {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|еҶҶеўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} || 60вҖ“70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || ЕҢtsuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' || Hotategaigata Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|ЕҢzono Kofun|ja|еӨ§ең’еҸӨеўі|12=draft|12=draft}} || Hotategaigata Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Togidensha Kofun|ja|еҜҢжңЁи»ҠеЎҡеҸӨеўі|12=draft|12=draft}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө|12=draft|12=draft}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј|12=draft|12=draft}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.Historic Site Intypeation Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the type of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|и‘әзҹі|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ|12=draft|12=draft}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[5th century]], during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј|12=draft|12=draft}}. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Historic Site]}} (Izumi City official website). File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Full view of the mound

Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө|12=draft|12=draft}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј|12=draft|12=draft}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.Historic Site Intypeation Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the type of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|и‘әзҹі|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ|12=draft|12=draft}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[5th century]], during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј|12=draft|12=draft}}. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Historic Site]}} (Izumi City official website). File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Full view of the mound

The mound is in the type of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|и‘әзҹі|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ|12=draft|12=draft}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[5th century]], during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј|12=draft|12=draft}}. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Historic Site]}} (Izumi City official website). File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Full view of the mound

It has been Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ6|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65вҖ“70. ! colspan="2" | part_of !! Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј|qq=draft|qq=draft}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.Historic Site Intypeation Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the type of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|и‘әзҹі|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[5th century]], during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Historic Site]}} (Izumi City official website). File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Full view of the mound

еўідёҳе…ҘгӮҠеҸЈгғ»иӘ¬жҳҺжқҝ |еҲҘеҗҚ = |жүҖеұһ = |жүҖеңЁең° = [[еӨ§йҳӘеәң]][[е’ҢжіүеёӮ]]еӨӘз”ә |з·ҜеәҰеәҰ = 34|з·ҜеәҰеҲҶ = 30|з·ҜеәҰз§’ = 30.40 |зөҢеәҰеәҰ =135|зөҢеәҰеҲҶ = 26|зөҢеәҰз§’ = 32.25 |ISO = JP-27 |еҪўзҠ¶ = [[еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі]] |иҰҸжЁЎ = еўідёҳй•·60m
й«ҳгҒ•7.7m |еҹӢи‘¬ж–ҪиЁӯ = дёҚжҳҺ |еҮәеңҹе“Ғ = ж»‘зҹіиЈҪж§Ҫгғ»[[еҹҙијӘ]]зүҮ |йҷӘеЎҡ = |зҜүйҖ жҷӮжңҹ = [[5дё–зҙҖ]]еүҚеҚҠ |иў«и‘¬иҖ… = |йҷөеў“ = |еҸІи·Ў = е’ҢжіүеёӮжҢҮе®ҡеҸІи·ЎгҖҢдҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўігҖҚ |жңүеҪўж–ҮеҢ–иІЎ = |зү№иЁҳдәӢй … = |ең°еӣі = Japan Osaka |гӮўгӮӨгӮігғі = еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі-еҢ—иҘҝ |гғ©гғҷгғ« = дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұ
еҸӨеўі |гғ©гғҷгғ«дҪҚзҪ® = top }} '''дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі'''пјҲгҒ—гҒ®гҒ гҒӢгҒ„гҒ¶гҒҚгӮ„гҒҫгҒ“гҒөгӮ“пјүгҒҜгҖҒ[[еӨ§йҳӘеәң]][[е’ҢжіүеёӮ]]еӨӘз”әгҒ«гҒӮгӮӢ[[еҸӨеўі]]гҖӮеҪўзҠ¶гҒҜ[[еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі]]гҖӮе’ҢжіүеёӮжҢҮе®ҡеҸІи·ЎгҒ«жҢҮе®ҡгҒ•гӮҢгҒҰгҒ„гӮӢгҖӮ == жҰӮиҰҒ ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+е’ҢжіүеҢ—йғЁгҒ®йҰ–й•·еў“зі»иӯңгҖҢе’ҢжіүеҢ—йғЁгҒ®йҰ–й•·зі»иӯңгҖҚгҖҺе’ҢжіүеёӮгҒ®иҖғеҸӨгғ»еҸӨд»Јгғ»дёӯдё– пјҲе’ҢжіүеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ6пјүгҖҸ е’ҢжіүеёӮеҸІз·ЁгҒ•гӮ“е§”е“Ўдјҡз·ЁгҖҒе’ҢжіүеёӮгғ»гҒҺгӮҮгҒҶгҒӣгҒ„гҖҒ2013е№ҙгҖҒpp. 65-70гҖӮ !colspan=2|зҫӨ!!еҸӨеўіеҗҚ!!еҪўзҠ¶!!иҰҸжЁЎ!!зҜүйҖ жҷӮжңҹ |- |еҢ—|| ||[[е’Ңжіүй»„йҮ‘еЎҡеҸӨеўі]]||[[еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі]]||94m||4cжң« |- | ||еҚ—||дёёз¬ еұұеҸӨеўі||еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі||96m||4cжң« |- | ||еҚ—||йҚӢеЎҡеҸӨеўі||[[еҶҶеўі]]||60-70m||5cеҲқ |- | ||еҚ—||зҺӢеЎҡеҸӨеўі||[[еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі]]||46m||5cдёӯ |- |еҢ—|| ||'''дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі'''||еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі||60m||5cеүҚ |- |еҢ—|| ||гӮ«гғӢгғӨеЎҡеҸӨеўі||еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі||дёҚжҳҺ||5cеҫҢ |- |еҢ—|| ||[[еӨ§ең’еҸӨеўі]]||еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі||50m||5cжң« |- |еҢ—|| ||[[еҜҢжңЁи»ҠеЎҡеҸӨеўі]]||еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі||48m||6cдёӯ |- | ||еҚ—||зӢҗеЎҡеҸӨеўі||еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі||58m||6cеҫҢ |}
еӨ§йҳӘеәңеҚ—йғЁгҖҒдҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷөеҢ—иҘҝйә“гҒ®дҪҺдҪҚж®өдёҳдёҠгҒ«зҜүйҖ гҒ•гӮҢгҒҹеҸӨеўігҒ§гҒӮгӮӢгҖӮгҖҢиІқеҗ№еұұгҖҚгҒ®еҸӨеўіеҗҚгҒҜгҖҒ[[жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј]]гҒ®дёҖжҸҶпјҲеҚғеҺҹйЁ’еӢ•пјүгҒ«гҒҠгҒ„гҒҰиҫІж°‘гҒҢжі•иһәиІқгӮ’еҗҲеӣігҒ«еҪ“ең°гҒ«йӣҶгҒҫгҒЈгҒҹгҒ“гҒЁгҒ«з”ұжқҘгҒҷгӮӢеҸІи·ЎиӘ¬жҳҺжқҝгҖӮгҖӮгҒ“гӮҢгҒҫгҒ§гҒ«йғЁеҲҶзҡ„гҒӘзҷәжҺҳиӘҝжҹ»гҒҢе®ҹж–ҪгҒ•гӮҢгҒҰгҒ„гӮӢ{{Sfn|иІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўіпјҲеҸӨеўіпјү|1989}}гҖӮ еўіеҪўгҒҜеүҚж–№йғЁгҒҢзҹӯе°ҸгҒӘеёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўгҒ®еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеҪўгҒ§гҖҒеүҚж–№йғЁгӮ’еҢ—иҘҝж–№еҗ‘гҒ«еҗ‘гҒ‘гӮӢгҖӮеўідёҳй•·гҒҜзҙ„60гғЎгғјгғҲгғ«гҖҒеҫҢеҶҶйғЁгҒҜзӣҙеҫ„50гғЎгғјгғҲгғ«гғ»й«ҳгҒ•7.7гғЎгғјгғҲгғ«гӮ’жё¬гӮӢгҖӮеўідёҳиЎЁйқўгҒ§гҒҜ[[и‘әзҹі]]гғ»[[еҹҙијӘ]]пјҲ[[еҶҶзӯ’еҹҙијӘ]]гғ»[[еҪўиұЎеҹҙијӘ]]пјүгҒҢиӘҚгӮҒгӮүгӮҢгӮӢгҒ»гҒӢгҖҒеўідёҳе‘ЁеӣІгҒ«гҒҜе‘Ёжҝ гҒҢе·ЎгӮүгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢ{{Sfn|иІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўіпјҲеҸӨеўіпјү|1989}}гҖӮеҹӢи‘¬ж–ҪиЁӯгҒҜжҳҺгӮүгҒӢгҒ§гҒӘгҒ„гҖӮеҮәеңҹе“ҒгҒ«гҒҜеҹҙијӘгҒ®гҒ»гҒӢж»‘зҹіиЈҪж§ҪгҒҢгҒӮгӮӢ{{Sfn|иІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўіпјҲеҸӨеўіпјү|1989}}гҖӮ зҜүйҖ жҷӮжңҹгҒҜгҖҒ[[еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј]]дёӯжңҹгҒ®[[5дё–зҙҖ]]еүҚеҚҠй ғгҒЁжҺЁе®ҡгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢгҖӮе’Ңжіүең°ж–№еҢ—йғЁгҒ§гҒҜйҰ–й•·еў“еҸӨеўігҒЁгҒ—гҒҰеҢ—зҫӨгғ»еҚ—зҫӨгҒ®2зҫӨгҒҢиӘҚгӮҒгӮүгӮҢгӮӢгҒҢгҖҒеҢ—зҫӨгғ»еҚ—зҫӨгҒ«гҒҜгҒқгӮҢгҒһгӮҢжҷӮжңҹзҡ„з©әзҷҪгҒҢиӘҚгӮҒгӮүгӮҢгӮӢгҒ“гҒЁгҒӢгӮүдёЎзҫӨгҒҢијӘз•ӘеҲ¶гҒ§йҰ–й•·гӮ’жӢ…гҒЈгҒҹгҒЁжҺЁе®ҡгҒ•гӮҢгҖҒжң¬еҸӨеўігҒҜгҒқгҒ®гҒҶгҒЎгҒ®1гҒӨгҒ«дҪҚзҪ®гҒҘгҒ‘гӮүгӮҢгӮӢгҖҺеӨ§ең’йҒәи·ЎзҷәжҺҳиӘҝжҹ»е ұе‘Ҡжӣё -еңҹең°еҢәз”»ж•ҙзҗҶдәӢжҘӯгҒ«дјҙгҒҶи‘ӣгҒ®и‘үең°еҢәгҒ®иӘҝжҹ»-гҖҸ е’ҢжіүеёӮж•ҷиӮІе§”е“ЎдјҡгҖҒ2014е№ҙгҖҒpp. 1-4гҖӮгҖӮ еҸӨеўіеҹҹгҒҜ[[2003е№ҙ]]пјҲ[[е№іжҲҗ]]15е№ҙпјүгҒ«е’ҢжіүеёӮжҢҮе®ҡеҸІи·ЎгҒ«жҢҮе®ҡгҒ•гӮҢгҒҰгҒ„гӮӢгҖӮ File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|еўідёҳе…ЁжҷҜ
{{small|дёӯеӨ®гҒ«еҫҢеҶҶйғЁгҖҒе·ҰгҒ«еүҚж–№йғЁгҖӮ}} == ж–ҮеҢ–иІЎ == === е’ҢжіүеёӮжҢҮе®ҡж–ҮеҢ–иІЎ === * еҸІи·Ў ** дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі - 2003е№ҙпјҲе№іжҲҗ15е№ҙпјү3жңҲ28ж—ҘжҢҮе®ҡ{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf е’ҢжіүеёӮгҒ®жҢҮе®ҡж–ҮеҢ–иІЎ]}}пјҲе’ҢжіүеёӮгғӣгғјгғ гғҡгғјгӮёпјүгҖӮгҖӮ == и„ҡжіЁ == {{и„ҡжіЁгғҳгғ«гғ—}} {{reflist}} == еҸӮиҖғж–ҮзҢ® == {{small|пјҲиЁҳдәӢеҹ·зӯҶгҒ«дҪҝз”ЁгҒ—гҒҹж–ҮзҢ®пјү}} * еҸІи·ЎиӘ¬жҳҺжқҝпјҲе’ҢжіүеёӮж•ҷиӮІе§”е“ЎдјҡгҖҒ2005е№ҙиЁӯзҪ®пјү * {{Cite book|е’Ңжӣё|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=иІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі|title=[[ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»]] 28 еӨ§йҳӘеәңгҒ®ең°еҗҚ|publisher=[[е№іеҮЎзӨҫ]]|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|иІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўіпјҲе№іеҮЎзӨҫпјү|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|е’Ңжӣё|editor=|author=еӨ§и°·зҢӣ|year=1989|chapter=иІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі|title=[[ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё]]|publisher=[[жқұдә¬е ӮеҮәзүҲ]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|иІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўіпјҲеҸӨеўіпјү|1989}}}} == й–ўйҖЈж–ҮзҢ® == {{small|пјҲиЁҳдәӢеҹ·зӯҶгҒ«дҪҝз”ЁгҒ—гҒҰгҒ„гҒӘгҒ„й–ўйҖЈж–ҮзҢ®пјү}} * {{Cite book|е’Ңжӣё|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі|title=е’ҢжіүеёӮеҹӢи”өж–ҮеҢ–иІЎзҷәжҺҳиӘҝжҹ»жҰӮе ұ26|publisher=е’ҢжіүеёӮж•ҷиӮІе§”е“Ўдјҡ|isbn=|ref=}} == еӨ–йғЁгғӘгғігӮҜ == {{Commonscat|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun}} * [https://izbun.jp/introduction/shinoda/shinodakaibukiyamakofun.html дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі] - е’ҢжіүеёӮж•ҷиӮІе§”е“Ўдјҡз”ҹж¶ҜеӯҰзҝ’йғЁж–ҮеҢ–иІЎжҢҜиҲҲиӘІгҖҢе’ҢжіүеёӮгҒ®ж–ҮеҢ–иІЎгҖҚ {{гғҮгғ•гӮ©гғ«гғҲгӮҪгғјгғҲ:гҒ—гҒ®гҒҹгҒӢгҒ„гҒөгҒҚгӮ„гҒҫгҒ“гҒөгӮ“}} [[Category:еӨ§йҳӘеәңгҒ®еҸӨеўі]] [[Category:е’ҢжіүеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ]] [[Category:еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі]]

{{ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®еҸӨеўі |еҗҚз§° = дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі |з”»еғҸ = [[File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
еўідёҳе…ҘгӮҠеҸЈгғ»иӘ¬жҳҺжқҝ |еҲҘеҗҚ = |жүҖеұһ = |жүҖеңЁең° = [[еӨ§йҳӘеәң]][[е’ҢжіүеёӮ]]еӨӘз”ә |з·ҜеәҰеәҰ = 34|з·ҜеәҰеҲҶ = 30|з·ҜеәҰз§’ = 30.40 |зөҢеәҰеәҰ =135|зөҢеәҰеҲҶ = 26|зөҢеәҰз§’ = 32.25 |ISO = JP-27 |еҪўзҠ¶ = [[еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі]] |иҰҸжЁЎ = еўідёҳй•·60m
й«ҳгҒ•7.7m |еҹӢи‘¬ж–ҪиЁӯ = дёҚжҳҺ |еҮәеңҹе“Ғ = ж»‘зҹіиЈҪж§Ҫгғ»[[еҹҙијӘ]]зүҮ |йҷӘеЎҡ = |зҜүйҖ жҷӮжңҹ = [[5дё–зҙҖ]]еүҚеҚҠ |иў«и‘¬иҖ… = |йҷөеў“ = |еҸІи·Ў = е’ҢжіүеёӮжҢҮе®ҡеҸІи·ЎгҖҢдҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўігҖҚ |жңүеҪўж–ҮеҢ–иІЎ = |зү№иЁҳдәӢй … = |ең°еӣі = Japan Osaka |гӮўгӮӨгӮігғі = еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі-еҢ—иҘҝ |гғ©гғҷгғ« = дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұ
еҸӨеўі |гғ©гғҷгғ«дҪҚзҪ® = top }} '''дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі'''пјҲгҒ—гҒ®гҒ гҒӢгҒ„гҒ¶гҒҚгӮ„гҒҫгҒ“гҒөгӮ“пјүгҒҜгҖҒ[[еӨ§йҳӘеәң]][[е’ҢжіүеёӮ]]еӨӘз”әгҒ«гҒӮгӮӢ[[еҸӨеўі]]гҖӮеҪўзҠ¶гҒҜ[[еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі]]гҖӮе’ҢжіүеёӮжҢҮе®ҡеҸІи·ЎгҒ«жҢҮе®ҡгҒ•гӮҢгҒҰгҒ„гӮӢгҖӮ == жҰӮиҰҒ ==

еӨ§йҳӘеәңеҚ—йғЁгҖҒдҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷөеҢ—иҘҝйә“гҒ®дҪҺдҪҚж®өдёҳдёҠгҒ«зҜүйҖ гҒ•гӮҢгҒҹеҸӨеўігҒ§гҒӮгӮӢгҖӮгҖҢиІқеҗ№еұұгҖҚгҒ®еҸӨеўіеҗҚгҒҜгҖҒ[[жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј]]гҒ®дёҖжҸҶпјҲеҚғеҺҹйЁ’еӢ•пјүгҒ«гҒҠгҒ„гҒҰиҫІж°‘гҒҢжі•иһәиІқгӮ’еҗҲеӣігҒ«еҪ“ең°гҒ«йӣҶгҒҫгҒЈгҒҹгҒ“гҒЁгҒ«з”ұжқҘгҒҷгӮӢеҸІи·ЎиӘ¬жҳҺжқҝгҖӮгҖӮгҒ“гӮҢгҒҫгҒ§гҒ«йғЁеҲҶзҡ„гҒӘзҷәжҺҳиӘҝжҹ»гҒҢе®ҹж–ҪгҒ•гӮҢгҒҰгҒ„гӮӢ{{Sfn|иІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўіпјҲеҸӨеўіпјү|1989}}гҖӮ еўіеҪўгҒҜеүҚж–№йғЁгҒҢзҹӯе°ҸгҒӘеёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўгҒ®еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеҪўгҒ§гҖҒеүҚж–№йғЁгӮ’еҢ—иҘҝж–№еҗ‘гҒ«еҗ‘гҒ‘гӮӢгҖӮеўідёҳй•·гҒҜзҙ„60гғЎгғјгғҲгғ«гҖҒеҫҢеҶҶйғЁгҒҜзӣҙеҫ„50гғЎгғјгғҲгғ«гғ»й«ҳгҒ•7.7гғЎгғјгғҲгғ«гӮ’жё¬гӮӢгҖӮеўідёҳиЎЁйқўгҒ§гҒҜ[[и‘әзҹі]]гғ»[[еҹҙијӘ]]пјҲ[[еҶҶзӯ’еҹҙијӘ]]гғ»[[еҪўиұЎеҹҙијӘ]]пјүгҒҢиӘҚгӮҒгӮүгӮҢгӮӢгҒ»гҒӢгҖҒеўідёҳе‘ЁеӣІгҒ«гҒҜе‘Ёжҝ гҒҢе·ЎгӮүгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢ{{Sfn|иІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўіпјҲеҸӨеўіпјү|1989}}гҖӮеҹӢи‘¬ж–ҪиЁӯгҒҜжҳҺгӮүгҒӢгҒ§гҒӘгҒ„гҖӮеҮәеңҹе“ҒгҒ«гҒҜеҹҙијӘгҒ®гҒ»гҒӢж»‘зҹіиЈҪж§ҪгҒҢгҒӮгӮӢ{{Sfn|иІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўіпјҲеҸӨеўіпјү|1989}}гҖӮ зҜүйҖ жҷӮжңҹгҒҜгҖҒ[[еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј]]дёӯжңҹгҒ®[[5дё–зҙҖ]]еүҚеҚҠй ғгҒЁжҺЁе®ҡгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢгҖӮе’Ңжіүең°ж–№еҢ—йғЁгҒ§гҒҜйҰ–й•·еў“еҸӨеўігҒЁгҒ—гҒҰеҢ—зҫӨгғ»еҚ—зҫӨгҒ®2зҫӨгҒҢиӘҚгӮҒгӮүгӮҢгӮӢгҒҢгҖҒеҢ—зҫӨгғ»еҚ—зҫӨгҒ«гҒҜгҒқгӮҢгҒһгӮҢжҷӮжңҹзҡ„з©әзҷҪгҒҢиӘҚгӮҒгӮүгӮҢгӮӢгҒ“гҒЁгҒӢгӮүдёЎзҫӨгҒҢијӘз•ӘеҲ¶гҒ§йҰ–й•·гӮ’жӢ…гҒЈгҒҹгҒЁжҺЁе®ҡгҒ•гӮҢгҖҒжң¬еҸӨеўігҒҜгҒқгҒ®гҒҶгҒЎгҒ®1гҒӨгҒ«дҪҚзҪ®гҒҘгҒ‘гӮүгӮҢгӮӢгҖҺеӨ§ең’йҒәи·ЎзҷәжҺҳиӘҝжҹ»е ұе‘Ҡжӣё -еңҹең°еҢәз”»ж•ҙзҗҶдәӢжҘӯгҒ«дјҙгҒҶи‘ӣгҒ®и‘үең°еҢәгҒ®иӘҝжҹ»-гҖҸ е’ҢжіүеёӮж•ҷиӮІе§”е“ЎдјҡгҖҒ2014е№ҙгҖҒpp. 1-4гҖӮгҖӮ еҸӨеўіеҹҹгҒҜ[[2003е№ҙ]]пјҲ[[е№іжҲҗ]]15е№ҙпјүгҒ«е’ҢжіүеёӮжҢҮе®ҡеҸІи·ЎгҒ«жҢҮе®ҡгҒ•гӮҢгҒҰгҒ„гӮӢгҖӮ File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|еўідёҳе…ЁжҷҜ

Height: 7.7m | burial = Unknown | burial goods = Steatite trough, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} fragments | period = Early [[5th century]] | Historic Site = Izumi City Historic Site (2003) }} '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' (дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Izumi, Osaka|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|еӨ§йҳӘеәң|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan. It has been Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ6|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65вҖ“70. ! colspan="2" | part_of !! Tomb name !! type !! area !! built |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|ja|е’Ңжіүй»„йҮ‘еЎҡеҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|| {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|еҶҶеўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} || 60вҖ“70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || ЕҢtsuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' || Hotategaigata Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|ЕҢzono Kofun|ja|еӨ§ең’еҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft}} || Hotategaigata Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Togidensha Kofun|ja|еҜҢжңЁи»ҠеЎҡеҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј|qq=draft|qq=draft}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.Historic Site Intypeation Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the type of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|и‘әзҹі|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[5th century]], during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Historic Site]}} (Izumi City official website). Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј|qq=draft|qq=draft}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.Historic Site Intypeation Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the type of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|и‘әзҹі|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[5th century]], during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Historic Site]}} (Izumi City official website). Height: 7.7m | burial = Unknown | burial goods = Steatite trough, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} fragments | period = Early [[5th century]] | Historic Site = Izumi City Historic Site (2003) }} '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' (дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Izumi, Osaka|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|еӨ§йҳӘеәң|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan. It has been Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ6|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65вҖ“70. ! colspan="2" | part_of !! Tomb name !! type !! area !! built |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|ja|е’Ңжіүй»„йҮ‘еЎҡеҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|| {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|еҶҶеўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} || 60вҖ“70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || ЕҢtsuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' || Hotategaigata Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|ЕҢzono Kofun|ja|еӨ§ең’еҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft}} || Hotategaigata Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Togidensha Kofun|ja|еҜҢжңЁи»ҠеЎҡеҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј|qq=draft|qq=draft}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.Historic Site Intypeation Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the type of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|и‘әзҹі|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[5th century]], during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Historic Site]}} (Izumi City official website). File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Full view of the mound

Historic Site of Izumi City] (Izumi City Education Board) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі|version=93272866|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Izumi, Osaka]] [[Category:Hotategaigata Kofun]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun]][[sn:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun]][[sn:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun]] [[ja:дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі]]

Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinodayama Hills|qq=}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.Historic Site Intypeation Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the type of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|и‘әзҹі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukiishi|qq=}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniwa|qq=}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[5th century]], during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Historic Site]}} (Izumi City official website). File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Full view of the mound
{{small|Center: rear circular section, left: front section.}} == Historic Site == === Izumi City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun вҖ“ Designated on March 28, 2003. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for this article)}} * Historic Site Intypeation Board (Izumi City Education Board, 2005) * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=ЕҢtani Takeshi|year=1989|chapter=Kaibukiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Kaibukiyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|year=1986|chapter=Kaibukiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Topography Series|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Topography Series|qq=}} Osaka Volume|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Kaibukiyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun}} * [https://izbun.jp/introduction/shinoda/shinodakaibukiyamakofun.html Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun - Historic Site of Izumi City] (Izumi City Education Board) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі|version=93272866|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Izumi, Osaka]] [[Category:Hotategaigata Kofun]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun]][[sn:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun]][[sn:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun]] [[ja:дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі]]

It has been Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ6|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi City History Volume 6|qq=}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65вҖ“70. ! colspan="2" | part_of !! Tomb name !! type !! area !! built |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|ja|е’Ңжіүй»„йҮ‘еЎҡеҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|qq=}}|| {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|еҶҶеўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} || 60вҖ“70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || ЕҢtsuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' || Hotategaigata Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|ЕҢzono Kofun|ja|еӨ§ең’еҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢzono Kofun|qq=}} || Hotategaigata Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Togidensha Kofun|ja|еҜҢжңЁи»ҠеЎҡеҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togidensha Kofun|qq=}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinodayama Hills|qq=}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.Historic Site Intypeation Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the type of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|и‘әзҹі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukiishi|qq=}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniwa|qq=}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[5th century]], during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Historic Site]}} (Izumi City official website). File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Full view of the mound

Historic Site of Izumi City] (Izumi City Education Board) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі|version=93272866|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Izumi, Osaka]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun]][[sn:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun]][[sn:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun]] [[ja:дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі]]

It has been Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ6|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi City History Volume 6|qq=}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65вҖ“70. ! colspan="2" | part_of !! Tomb name !! type !! area !! built |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|ja|е’Ңжіүй»„йҮ‘еЎҡеҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|qq=}}|| {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|еҶҶеўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} || 60вҖ“70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || ЕҢtsuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' || Hotategaigata Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Ozono Kofun|lt=Ozono Kofun|lt=ЕҢzono Kofun|ja|еӨ§ең’еҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢzono Kofun|qq=}} || Hotategaigata Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Togidensha Kofun|ja|еҜҢжңЁи»ҠеЎҡеҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togidensha Kofun|qq=}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinodayama Hills|qq=}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.Historic Site Intypeation Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the type of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|и‘әзҹі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukiishi|qq=}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniwa|qq=}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[5th century]], during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Historic Site]}} (Izumi City official website). File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Full view of the mound
