Historic Site of Izumi City] (Izumi City Education Board) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі|version=93272866|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Izumi, Osaka]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun]][[sn:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun]][[sn:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun]] [[ja:дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі]]

It has been Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City. == Overview ==

Tomb name !! type !! area !! built |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|ja|е’Ңжіүй»„йҮ‘еЎҡеҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|qq=}}|| {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі|4=ko|5=м „л°©нӣ„мӣҗл¶„|6=zh|7=еүҚж–№еҫҢең“еўі|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|еҶҶеўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} || 60вҖ“70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || ЕҢtsuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' || Hotategaigata Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Ozono Kofun|lt=Ozono Kofun|lt=ЕҢzono Kofun|ja|еӨ§ең’еҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢzono Kofun|qq=}} || Hotategaigata Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|lt=Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|lt=Togidensha Kofun|ja|еҜҢжңЁи»ҠеЎҡеҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togidensha Kofun|qq=}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinodayama Hills|qq=}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.Historic Site Intypeation Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the type of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|и‘әзҹі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukiishi|qq=}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniwa|qq=}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[5th century]], during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Historic Site]}} (Izumi City official website). File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Full view of the mound

Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]] and [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Historic Site]}} (Izumi City official website). Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the type of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]] and [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[5th century]], during the middle period of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Historic Site]}} (Izumi City official website). {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ6|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi City History Volume 6|qq=}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65вҖ“70. ! colspan="2" | part_of !! Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinodayama Hills|qq=}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.Historic Site Intypeation Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the type of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]] and [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[5th century]], during the middle period of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Historic Site]}} (Izumi City official website). File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Full view of the mound

Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}} | name = Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun (дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі) | image = [[File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the mound and intypeational sign | alternate_name = | part_of = | location = [[:en:Izumi, Osaka|Izumi, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|30.40|N|135|26|32.25|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Hotategaigata Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategaigata Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}} | area = Total mound length: 60m
Height: 7.7m | burial = Unknown | burial goods = Steatite trough, [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] fragments | period = Early [[5th century]] | Historic Site = Izumi City Historic Site (2003) }} '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' (дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}} located in [[:en:Izumi, Osaka|Izumi, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. It has been Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ6|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi City History Volume 6|qq=}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65вҖ“70. ! colspan="2" | part_of !! Tomb name !! type !! area !! built |- | North || || [[:en:Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun]]|| [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|еҶҶеўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} || 60вҖ“70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || ЕҢtsuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' || Hotategaigata Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Ozono Kofun|lt=Ozono Kofun|lt=ЕҢzono Kofun|ja|еӨ§ең’еҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢzono Kofun|qq=}} || Hotategaigata Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|lt=Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|lt=Togidensha Kofun|ja|еҜҢжңЁи»ҠеЎҡеҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togidensha Kofun|qq=}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinodayama Hills|qq=}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.Historic Site Intypeation Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the type of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]] and [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[5th century]], during the middle period of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Historic Site]}} (Izumi City official website). File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Full view of the mound
{{small|Center: rear circular section, left: front section.}} == Historic Site == === Izumi City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun вҖ“ Designated on March 28, 2003. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for this article)}} * Historic Site Intypeation Board (Izumi City Education Board, 2005) * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=ЕҢtani Takeshi|year=1989|chapter=Kaibukiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Kaibukiyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|year=1986|chapter=Kaibukiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Topography Series|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Topography Series|qq=}} Osaka Volume|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Kaibukiyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun}} * [https://izbun.jp/introduction/shinoda/shinodakaibukiyamakofun.html Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun - Historic Site of Izumi City] (Izumi City Education Board) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі|version=93272866|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Izumi, Osaka]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun]][[sn:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun]][[sn:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun]] [[ja:дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі]] [[Category:е’ҢжіүеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ]] [[Category:еӨ§йҳӘеәңгҒ®еҸӨеўі]] {{Commons category|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun}}

Height: 7.7m | burial = Unknown | burial goods = Steatite trough, [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] fragments | period = Early [[5th century]] | Historic Site = Izumi City Historic Site (2003) }} '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' (дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}} located in [[:en:Izumi, Osaka|Izumi, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. It has been Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ6|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi City History Volume 6|qq=}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65вҖ“70. ! colspan="2" | part_of !! Tomb name !! type !! area !! built |- | North || || [[:en:Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun]]|| [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|еҶҶеўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} || 60вҖ“70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || ЕҢtsuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' || Hotategaigata Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Ozono Kofun|lt=Ozono Kofun|lt=ЕҢzono Kofun|ja|еӨ§ең’еҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢzono Kofun|qq=}} || Hotategaigata Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|lt=Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|lt=Togidensha Kofun|ja|еҜҢжңЁи»ҠеЎҡеҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togidensha Kofun|qq=}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinodayama Hills|qq=}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.Historic Site Intypeation Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the type of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]] and [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[5th century]], during the middle period of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Historic Site]}} (Izumi City official website). File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Full view of the mound

{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ6|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi City History Volume 6|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65вҖ“70. ! colspan="2" | part_of !! Tomb name !! type !! area !! built |- | North || || [[:en:Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun]]|| [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|еҶҶеўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} || 60вҖ“70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || ЕҢtsuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' || Hotategaigata Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Ozono Kofun|lt=Ozono Kofun|lt=ЕҢzono Kofun|ja|еӨ§ең’еҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢzono Kofun|qq=}} || Hotategaigata Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|lt=Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|lt=Togidensha Kofun|ja|еҜҢжңЁи»ҠеЎҡеҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togidensha Kofun|qq=}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinodayama Hills|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎеӨӘеұұжј”зҝ’е ҙ}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.Historic Site Intypeation Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the type of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]] and [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[5th century]], during the middle period of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Historic Site]}} (Izumi City official website). File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Full view of the mound
{{small|Center: rear circular section, left: front section.}} == Historic Site == === Izumi City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun вҖ“ Designated on March 28, 2003. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for this article)}} * Historic Site Intypeation Board (Izumi City Education Board, 2005) * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=ЕҢtani Takeshi|year=1989|chapter=Kaibukiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Kaibukiyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|year=1986|chapter=Kaibukiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Topography Series|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Topography Series|qq=}} Osaka Volume|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Kaibukiyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun}} * [https://izbun.jp/introduction/shinoda/shinodakaibukiyamakofun.html Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun - Historic Site of Izumi City] (Izumi City Education Board) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі|version=93272866|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Izumi, Osaka]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun]][[sn:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun]][[sn:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun]] [[ja:дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі]] [[Category:History of Izumi, Osaka]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] {{Commons category|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun}}

"Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the type of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]] and [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[:en:5th century|5th century]], during the middle period of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Historic Site]}} (Izumi City official website). File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Full view of the mound
{{small|Center: rear circular section, left: front section.}} == Historic Site == === Izumi City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun вҖ“ Designated on March 28, 2003. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for this article)}} * Historic Site Intypeation Board (Izumi City Education Board, 2005) * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=ЕҢtani Takeshi|year=1989|chapter=Kaibukiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Kaibukiyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|year=1986|chapter=Kaibukiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Topography Series|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Topography Series|qq=}} Osaka Volume|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Kaibukiyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun}} * [https://izbun.jp/introduction/shinoda/shinodakaibukiyamakofun.html Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun - Historic Site of Izumi City] (Izumi City Education Board) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі|version=93272866|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Izumi, Osaka]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun]][[sn:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun]][[sn:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun]] [[ja:дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі]] [[Category:History of Izumi, Osaka]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] {{Commons category|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun}}

Height: 7.7m | burial = Unknown | burial goods = Steatite trough, [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] fragments | period = Early [[:en:5th century|5th century]] | Historic Site = Izumi City Historic Site (2003) }} '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' (дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Izumi, Osaka|Izumi, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. It has been Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ6|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi City History Volume 6|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65вҖ“70. ! colspan="2" | part_of !! Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinodayama Hills|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎеӨӘеұұжј”зҝ’е ҙ}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.Historic Site Intypeation Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the type of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]] and [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[:en:5th century|5th century]], during the middle period of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Historic Site]}} (Izumi City official website). File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Full view of the mound

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun (дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі) | image = [[File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the mound and intypeational sign | alternate_name = | part_of = | location = [[:en:Izumi, Osaka|Izumi, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|30.40|N|135|26|32.25|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Hotategaigata Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategaigata Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Total mound length: 60m
Height: 7.7m | burial = Unknown | burial goods = Steatite trough, [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] fragments | period = Early [[:en:5th century|5th century]] | Historic Site = Izumi City Historic Site (2003) }} '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' (дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Izumi, Osaka|Izumi, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. It has been Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|е’ҢжіүеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ6|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi City History Volume 6|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65вҖ“70. ! colspan="2" | part_of !! Tomb name !! type !! area !! built |- | North || || [[:en:Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun]]|| [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|еҶҶеўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} || 60вҖ“70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || ЕҢtsuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' || Hotategaigata Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Ozono Kofun|lt=Ozono Kofun|lt=ЕҢzono Kofun|ja|еӨ§ең’еҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢzono Kofun|qq=}} || Hotategaigata Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|lt=Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|lt=Togidensha Kofun|ja|еҜҢжңЁи»ҠеЎҡеҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togidensha Kofun|qq=}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinodayama Hills|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎеӨӘеұұжј”зҝ’е ҙ}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.Historic Site Intypeation Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the type of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]] and [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[:en:5th century|5th century]], during the middle period of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Historic Site]}} (Izumi City official website). File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Full view of the mound

Height: 7.7m | burial = Unknown | burial goods = Steatite trough, [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] fragments | period = Early [[:en:5th century|5th century]] | Historic Site = Izumi City Historic Site (2003) }} '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' (дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Izumi, Osaka|Izumi, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. It has been Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|е’ҢжіүгҒ® Municipal History6|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi City History Volume 6|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65вҖ“70. ! colspan="2" | part_of !! Tomb name !! type !! area !! built |- | North || || [[:en:Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun]]|| [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|еҶҶеўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} || 60вҖ“70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || ЕҢtsuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' || Hotategaigata Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Ozono Kofun|lt=Ozono Kofun|lt=ЕҢzono Kofun|ja|еӨ§ең’еҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢzono Kofun|qq=}} || Hotategaigata Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|lt=Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|lt=Togidensha Kofun|ja|еҜҢжңЁи»ҠеЎҡеҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togidensha Kofun|qq=}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinodayama Hills|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎеӨӘеұұжј”зҝ’е ҙ}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.Historic Site Intypeation Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the type of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]] and [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[:en:5th century|5th century]], during the middle period of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Historic Site]}} (Izumi City official website). File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Full view of the mound

Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[:en:5th century|5th century]], during the middle period of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. Tomb name !! type !! area !! built |- | North || || [[:en:Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun]]|| [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|еҶҶеўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} || 60вҖ“70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || ЕҢtsuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' || Hotategaigata Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Ozono Kofun|lt=Ozono Kofun|lt=ЕҢzono Kofun|ja|еӨ§ең’еҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢzono Kofun|qq=}} || Hotategaigata Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|lt=Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|lt=Togidensha Kofun|ja|еҜҢжңЁи»ҠеЎҡеҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togidensha Kofun|qq=}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinodayama Hills|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎеӨӘеұұжј”зҝ’е ҙ}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.Historic Site Intypeation Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the type of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=scallop-shaped kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]] and [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[:en:5th century|5th century]], during the middle period of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Historic Site]}} (Izumi City official website). It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]] and [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[:en:5th century|5th century]], during the middle period of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Historic Site]}} (Izumi City official website). File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Full view of the mound

It has been Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|е’ҢжіүгҒ® Municipal History6|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi City History Volume 6|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65вҖ“70. ! colspan="2" | part_of !! Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinodayama Hills|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎеӨӘеұұжј”зҝ’е ҙ}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.Historic Site Intypeation Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the type of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=scallop-shaped kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]] and [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[:en:5th century|5th century]], during the middle period of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Historic Site]}} (Izumi City official website). File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Full view of the mound
{{small|Center: rear circular section, left: front section.}} == Historic Site == === Izumi City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun вҖ“ Designated on March 28, 2003. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for this article)}} * Historic Site Intypeation Board (Izumi City Education Board, 2005) * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=ЕҢtani Takeshi|year=1989|chapter=Kaibukiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Kaibukiyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|year=1986|chapter=Kaibukiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Topography Series|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Topography Series|qq=}} Osaka Volume|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Kaibukiyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun}} * [https://izbun.jp/introduction/shinoda/shinodakaibukiyamakofun.html Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun - It has been Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|е’ҢжіүгҒ® Municipal History6|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi City History Volume 6|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65вҖ“70. ! colspan="2" | part_of !! Tomb name !! type !! area !! built |- | North || || [[:en:Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun]]|| [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|еҶҶеўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} || 60вҖ“70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || ЕҢtsuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' || Hotategaigata Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Ozono Kofun|lt=Ozono Kofun|lt=ЕҢzono Kofun|ja|еӨ§ең’еҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢzono Kofun|qq=}} || Hotategaigata Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|lt=Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|lt=Togidensha Kofun|ja|еҜҢжңЁи»ҠеЎҡеҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togidensha Kofun|qq=}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinodayama Hills|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎеӨӘеұұжј”зҝ’е ҙ}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.Historic Site Intypeation Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the type of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=scallop-shaped kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]] and [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[:en:5th century|5th century]], during the middle period of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Historic Site]}} (Izumi City official website). File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Full view of the mound

Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinodayama Hills|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎеӨӘеұұжј”зҝ’е ҙ}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.Historic Site Intypeation Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the type of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=scallop-shaped kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]] and [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[:en:5th century|5th century]], during the middle period of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Historic Site]}} (Izumi City official website). This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun (дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі) | image = [[File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the mound and intypeational sign | alternate_name = | part_of = | location = [[:en:Izumi, Osaka|Izumi, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34.508444|N|135.442292|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Hotategaigata Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=scallop-shaped kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategaigata Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Total mound length: 60m
Height: 7.7m | burial = Unknown | burial goods = Steatite trough, [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] fragments | period = Early [[:en:5th century|5th century]] | Historic Site = Izumi City Historic Site (2003) | architectural_styles = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|qid=Q11480883}} }} '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' (дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=scallop-shaped kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Izumi, Osaka|Izumi, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. It has been Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|е’ҢжіүгҒ® Municipal History6|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi City History Volume 6|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65вҖ“70. ! colspan="2" | part_of !! Tomb name !! type !! area !! built |- | North || || [[:en:Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun]]|| [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|еҶҶеўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} || 60вҖ“70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || ЕҢtsuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' || Hotategaigata Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Ozono Kofun|lt=Ozono Kofun|lt=ЕҢzono Kofun|ja|еӨ§ең’еҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢzono Kofun|qq=}} || Hotategaigata Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|lt=Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|lt=Togidensha Kofun|ja|еҜҢжңЁи»ҠеЎҡеҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togidensha Kofun|qq=}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinodayama Hills|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎеӨӘеұұжј”зҝ’е ҙ}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.Historic Site Intypeation Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the type of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=scallop-shaped kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]] and [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[:en:5th century|5th century]], during the middle period of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Historic Site]}} (Izumi City official website). File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Full view of the mound
{{small|Center: rear circular section, left: front section.}} == Historic Site == === Izumi City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun вҖ“ Designated on March 28, 2003. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for this article)}} * Historic Site Intypeation Board (Izumi City Education Board, 2005) * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=ЕҢtani Takeshi|year=1989|chapter=Kaibukiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Kaibukiyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|year=1986|chapter=Kaibukiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Topography Series|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Topography Series|qq=}} Osaka Volume|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Kaibukiyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun}} * [https://izbun.jp/introduction/shinoda/shinodakaibukiyamakofun.html Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun - Historic Site of Izumi City] (Izumi City Education Board) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі|version=93272866|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Izumi, Osaka]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[sn:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun]][[sn:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun]][[sn:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun]] [[ja:дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі]] [[Category:History of Izumi, Osaka]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] {{Commons category|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun}} [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[en:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun]] {{wikidata link|Q55535982}}

Tomb name !! type !! area !! built |- | North || || [[:en:Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun]]|| [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|еҶҶеўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} || 60вҖ“70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || ЕҢtsuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' || Hotategaigata Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Ozono Kofun|lt=Ozono Kofun|lt=ЕҢzono Kofun|ja|еӨ§ең’еҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢzono Kofun|qq=}} || Hotategaigata Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|lt=Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|lt=Togidensha Kofun|ja|еҜҢжңЁи»ҠеЎҡеҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togidensha Kofun|qq=}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinodayama Hills|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎеӨӘеұұжј”зҝ’е ҙ}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.Historic Site Intypeation Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the type of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=scallop-shaped kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]] and [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[:en:5th century|5th century]], during the middle period of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Historic Site]}} (Izumi City official website). File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Full view of the mound

Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[:en:5th century|5th century]], during the middle period of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Historic Site]}} (Izumi City official website). Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early [[:en:5th century|5th century]], during the middle period of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Historic Site]}} (Izumi City official website).
