Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinodayama Hills|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎеӨӘеұұжј”зҝ’е ҙ}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.Historic Site Intypeation Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the type of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=Fukiishi}} and {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century}}, during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Historic Site]}} (Izumi City official website). Entrance to the mound and intypeational sign | alternate_name = | part_of = | location = {{ill|Izumi, Osaka|en|Izumi, Osaka|WD=Q696412|lt=Izumi, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34.508444|N|135.442292|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Hotategaigata Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategaigata Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Total mound length: 60m
Height: 7.7m | burial = Unknown | burial goods = Steatite trough, {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}} fragments | period = Early {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century}} | Historic Site = Izumi City Historic Site (2003) | architectural_styles = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|WD=Q11480883}} }} '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' (дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in {{ill|Izumi, Osaka|en|Izumi, Osaka|WD=Q696412|lt=Izumi, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan. It has been Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|е’ҢжіүгҒ® Municipal History6|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi City History Volume 6|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65вҖ“70. ! colspan="2" | part_of !! Tomb name !! type !! area !! built |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|en|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|WD=Q11417846|lt=Izumi Koganezuka Kofun}}|| {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|еҶҶеўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} || 60вҖ“70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || ЕҢtsuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' || Hotategaigata Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Ozono Kofun|lt=Ozono Kofun|lt=ЕҢzono Kofun|ja|еӨ§ең’еҸӨеўі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢzono Kofun|qq=}} || Hotategaigata Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|lt=Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|lt=Togidensha Kofun|ja|еҜҢжңЁи»ҠеЎҡеҸӨеўі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togidensha Kofun|qq=}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinodayama Hills|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎеӨӘеұұжј”зҝ’е ҙ}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.Historic Site Intypeation Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the type of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=Fukiishi}} and {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century}}, during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Historic Site]}} (Izumi City official website). File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Full view of the mound

Entrance to the mound and intypeational sign | alternate_name = | part_of = | location = {{ill|Izumi, Osaka|en|Izumi, Osaka|WD=Q696412|lt=Izumi, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34.508444|N|135.442292|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Hotategaigata Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategaigata Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Total mound length: 60m
Height: 7.7m | burial = Unknown | burial goods = Steatite trough, {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}} fragments | period = Early {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century}} | Historic Site = Izumi City Historic Site (2003) | architectural_styles = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|WD=Q11480883}} }} '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' (дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in {{ill|Izumi, Osaka|en|Izumi, Osaka|WD=Q696412|lt=Izumi, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan. It has been Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|е’ҢжіүгҒ® Municipal History6|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi City History Volume 6|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65вҖ“70. !part_of !Tomb name !type !area !built |- |North |{{ill|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|en|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|WD=Q11417846|lt=Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun}} |{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} |94m |Late 4th century |- |South |Marukasa-yama Kofun |{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} |96m |Late 4th century |- |South |Nabetsuka Kofun |{{ill|Round Kofun|ja|еҶҶеўі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |60вҖ“70m |Early 5th century |- |South |ЕҢtsuka Kofun |{{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі}} |46m |Mid-5th century |- |North |{{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|qq=}} |{{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі}} |60m |Early 5th century |- |North |Kaniya-zuka Kofun |{{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі}} |Unknown |Late 5th century |- |North |'''ЕҢzono Kofun''' |{{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі}} |50m |Late 5th century |- |North |{{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|еҜҢжңЁи»ҠеЎҡеҸӨеўі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=}} |{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} |48m |Mid-6th century |- |South |Kitsunezuka Kofun |{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} |58m |Late 6th century |}
Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinodayama Hills|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎеӨӘеұұжј”зҝ’е ҙ}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.Historic Site Intypeation Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the type of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=Fukiishi}} and {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century}}, during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}."Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|е’ҢжіүгҒ® Municipal History6|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi City History Volume 6|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65вҖ“70. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Historic Site]}} (Izumi City official website). File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Full view of the mound

Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinodayama Hills|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎеӨӘеұұжј”зҝ’е ҙ}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.Historic Site Intypeation Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the type of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=Fukiishi}} and {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Historic Site]}} (Izumi City official website). Height: 7.7m | burial = Unknown | burial goods = Steatite trough, {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}} fragments | period = Early {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century}} | Historic Site = Izumi City Historic Site (2003) | architectural_styles = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|WD=Q11480883}} }} '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' (дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in {{ill|Izumi, Osaka|en|Izumi, Osaka|WD=Q696412|lt=Izumi, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan. It has been Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|е’ҢжіүгҒ® Municipal History6|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi City History Volume 6|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65вҖ“70. !part_of !Tomb name !type !area !built |- |North |{{ill|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|en|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|WD=Q11417846|lt=Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun}} |{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} |94m |Late 4th century |- |South |Marukasa-yama Kofun |{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} |96m |Late 4th century |- |South |Nabetsuka Kofun |{{ill|Round Kofun|ja|еҶҶеўі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |60вҖ“70m |Early 5th century |- |South |ЕҢtsuka Kofun |{{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі}} |46m |Mid-5th century |- |North |{{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|qq=}} |{{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі}} |60m |Early 5th century |- |North |Kaniya-zuka Kofun |{{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі}} |Unknown |Late 5th century |- |North |'''ЕҢzono Kofun''' |{{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі}} |50m |Late 5th century |- |North |{{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|еҜҢжңЁи»ҠеЎҡеҸӨеўі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=}} |{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} |48m |Mid-6th century |- |South |Kitsunezuka Kofun |{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} |58m |Late 6th century |}
Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinodayama Hills|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎеӨӘеұұжј”зҝ’е ҙ}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.Historic Site Intypeation Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the type of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=Fukiishi}} and {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century}}, during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}."Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|е’ҢжіүгҒ® Municipal History6|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi City History Volume 6|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65вҖ“70. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Historic Site]}} (Izumi City official website). File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Full view of the mound
{{small|Center: rear circular section, left: front section.}} == Historic Site == === Izumi City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun вҖ“ Designated on March 28, 2003. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for this article)}} * Historic Site Intypeation Board (Izumi City Education Board, 2005) * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=ЕҢtani Takeshi|year=1989|chapter=Kaibukiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Kaibukiyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|year=1986|chapter=Kaibukiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Topography Series|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Topography Series|qq=}} Osaka Volume|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Kaibukiyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun}} * [https://izbun.jp/introduction/shinoda/shinodakaibukiyamakofun.html Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun - Historic Site of Izumi City] (Izumi City Education Board) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі|version=93272866|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі]] {{Commons category|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q55535982}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Izumi, Osaka]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinodayama Hills|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎеӨӘеұұжј”зҝ’е ҙ}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.Historic Site Intypeation Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the type of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=Fukiishi}} and {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century}}, during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}."Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|е’ҢжіүгҒ® Municipal History6|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi City History Volume 6|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65вҖ“70. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Historic Site]}} (Izumi City official website). It has been Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|е’ҢжіүгҒ® Municipal History6|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi City History Volume 6|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65вҖ“70. !part_of !Tomb name !type !area !built |- |North |{{ill|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|en|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|WD=Q11417846|lt=Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun|1=Izumi Koganezuka Kofun}} |{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=ZenpЕҚkЕҚenfun}} |94m |Late 4th century |- |South |Marukasa-yama Kofun |{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=ZenpЕҚkЕҚenfun}} |96m |Late 4th century |- |South |Nabetsuka Kofun |{{ill|Round Kofun|ja|еҶҶеўі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |60вҖ“70m |Early 5th century |- |South |ЕҢtsuka Kofun |{{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі}} |46m |Mid-5th century |- |North |{{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|qq=}} |{{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі}} |60m |Early 5th century |- |North |Kaniya-zuka Kofun |{{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі}} |Unknown |Late 5th century |- |North |'''ЕҢzono Kofun''' |{{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі}} |50m |Late 5th century |- |North |{{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|еҜҢжңЁи»ҠеЎҡеҸӨеўі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=}} |{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=ZenpЕҚkЕҚenfun}} |48m |Mid-6th century |- |South |Kitsunezuka Kofun |{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=ZenpЕҚkЕҚenfun}} |58m |Late 6th century |}
Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinodayama Hills|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎеӨӘеұұжј”зҝ’е ҙ}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.Historic Site Intypeation Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the type of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=Fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}} and {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}}, during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}."Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|е’ҢжіүгҒ® Municipal History6|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi City History Volume 6|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65вҖ“70. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Historic Site]}} (Izumi City official website). File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Full view of the mound

It has been Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|е’ҢжіүгҒ® Municipal History6|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi City History Volume 6|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65вҖ“70. !part_of !Tomb name !type !area !built |- |North |{{ill|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|en|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|WD=Q11417846|lt=Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun|1=Izumi Koganezuka Kofun}} |{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=ZenpЕҚkЕҚenfun}} |94m |Late 4th century |- |South |Marukasa-yama Kofun |{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=ZenpЕҚkЕҚenfun}} |96m |Late 4th century |- |South |Nabetsuka Kofun |{{ill|Round Kofun|ja|еҶҶеўі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |60вҖ“70m |Early 5th century |- |South |ЕҢtsuka Kofun |{{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі}} |46m |Mid-5th century |- |North |{{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|qq=}} |{{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі}} |60m |Early 5th century |- |North |Kaniya-zuka Kofun |{{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі}} |Unknown |Late 5th century |- |North |'''ЕҢzono Kofun''' |{{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі}} |50m |Late 5th century |- |North |{{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|еҜҢжңЁи»ҠеЎҡеҸӨеўі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=}} |{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=ZenpЕҚkЕҚenfun}} |48m |Mid-6th century |- |South |Kitsunezuka Kofun |{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=ZenpЕҚkЕҚenfun}} |58m |Late 6th century |}
Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinodayama Hills|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎеӨӘеұұжј”зҝ’е ҙ}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.Historic Site Intypeation Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the type of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=Fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}} and {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}}, during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}."Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|е’ҢжіүгҒ® Municipal History6|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi City History Volume 6|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65вҖ“70. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Historic Site]}} (Izumi City official website). File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Full view of the mound
{{small|Center: rear circular section, left: front section.}} == Historic Site == === Izumi City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun вҖ“ Designated on March 28, 2003. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for this article)}} * Historic Site Intypeation Board (Izumi City Education Board, 2005) * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=ЕҢtani Takeshi|year=1989|chapter=Kaibukiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Kaibukiyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|year=1986|chapter=Kaibukiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Topography Series|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Topography Series|qq=}} Osaka Volume|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Kaibukiyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun}} * [https://izbun.jp/introduction/shinoda/shinodakaibukiyamakofun.html Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun - Historic Site of Izumi City] (Izumi City Education Board) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі|version=93272866|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі]] {{Commons category|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q55535982}} [[Category:History of Izumi, Osaka]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

Height: 7.7m | burial = Unknown | burial goods = Steatite trough, {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} fragments | period = Early {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}} | Historic Site = Izumi City Historic Site (2003) | architectural_styles = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|WD=Q11480883|1=Scallop-shaped kofun}} }} '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' (дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in {{ill|Izumi, Osaka|en|Izumi, Osaka|WD=Q696412|lt=Izumi, Osaka|1=Izumi, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan. It has been Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|е’ҢжіүгҒ® Municipal History6|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi City History Volume 6|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65вҖ“70. !part_of !Tomb name !type !area !built |- |North |{{ill|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|en|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|WD=Q11417846|lt=Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun|1=Izumi Koganezuka Kofun}} |{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=ZenpЕҚkЕҚenfun}} |94m |Late 4th century |- |South |Marukasa-yama Kofun |{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=ZenpЕҚkЕҚenfun}} |96m |Late 4th century |- |South |Nabetsuka Kofun |{{ill|Round Kofun|ja|еҶҶеўі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |60вҖ“70m |Early 5th century |- |South |ЕҢtsuka Kofun |{{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі}} |46m |Mid-5th century |- |North |{{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|qq=}} |{{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі}} |60m |Early 5th century |- |North |Kaniya-zuka Kofun |{{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі}} |Unknown |Late 5th century |- |North |'''ЕҢzono Kofun''' |{{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі}} |50m |Late 5th century |- |North |{{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|еҜҢжңЁи»ҠеЎҡеҸӨеўі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=}} |{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=ZenpЕҚkЕҚenfun}} |48m |Mid-6th century |- |South |Kitsunezuka Kofun |{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=ZenpЕҚkЕҚenfun}} |58m |Late 6th century |}
Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinodayama Hills|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎеӨӘеұұжј”зҝ’е ҙ}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.Historic Site Intypeation Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the type of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=Fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}} and {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}}, during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}."Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|е’ҢжіүгҒ® Municipal History6|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi City History Volume 6|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65вҖ“70. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Historic Site]}} (Izumi City official website). File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Full view of the mound
{{small|Center: rear circular section, left: front section.}} == Historic Site == === Izumi City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun вҖ“ Designated on March 28, 2003. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for this article)}} * Historic Site Intypeation Board (Izumi City Education Board, 2005) * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=ЕҢtani Takeshi|year=1989|chapter=Kaibukiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Kaibukiyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|year=1986|chapter=Kaibukiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Topography Series|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Topography Series|qq=}} Osaka Volume|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Kaibukiyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1986}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun}} * [https://izbun.jp/introduction/shinoda/shinodakaibukiyamakofun.html Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun - Historic Site of Izumi City] (Izumi City Education Board) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі|version=93272866|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі]] {{Commons category|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q55535982}} [[Category:History of Izumi, Osaka]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

Height: 7.7m | burial = Unknown | burial goods = Steatite trough, {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} fragments | period = Early {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}} | Historic Site = Izumi City Historic Site (2003) | architectural_styles = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|WD=Q11480883|1=Scallop-shaped kofun}} }} '''Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun''' (дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in {{ill|Izumi, Osaka|en|Izumi, Osaka|WD=Q696412|lt=Izumi, Osaka|1=Izumi, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan. It has been Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|е’ҢжіүгҒ® Municipal History6|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi City History Volume 6|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65вҖ“70. !part_of !Tomb name !type !area !built |- |North |{{ill|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|en|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|WD=Q11417846|lt=Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun|1=Izumi Koganezuka Kofun}} |{{ill|ZenpЕҚ-kЕҚen-fun|en|ZenpЕҚ-kЕҚen-fun|WD=Q11268718|lt=ZenpЕҚ-kЕҚen-fun|1=ZenpЕҚ-kЕҚen-fun}} |94m |Late 4th century |- |South |Marukasa-yama Kofun |{{ill|ZenpЕҚ-kЕҚen-fun|en|ZenpЕҚ-kЕҚen-fun|WD=Q11268718|lt=ZenpЕҚ-kЕҚen-fun|1=ZenpЕҚ-kЕҚen-fun}} |96m |Late 4th century |- |South |Nabetsuka Kofun |{{ill|Round Kofun|ja|еҶҶеўі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |60вҖ“70m |Early 5th century |- |South |ЕҢtsuka Kofun |{{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі}} |46m |Mid-5th century |- |North |{{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|дҝЎеӨӘиІқеҗ№еұұеҸӨеўі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|qq=}} |{{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі}} |60m |Early 5th century |- |North |Kaniya-zuka Kofun |{{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі}} |Unknown |Late 5th century |- |North |'''ЕҢzono Kofun''' |{{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі}} |50m |Late 5th century |- |North |{{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|еҜҢжңЁи»ҠеЎҡеҸӨеўі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=}} |{{ill|ZenpЕҚ-kЕҚen-fun|en|ZenpЕҚ-kЕҚen-fun|WD=Q11268718|lt=ZenpЕҚ-kЕҚen-fun|1=ZenpЕҚ-kЕҚen-fun}} |48m |Mid-6th century |- |South |Kitsunezuka Kofun |{{ill|ZenpЕҚ-kЕҚen-fun|en|ZenpЕҚ-kЕҚen-fun|WD=Q11268718|lt=ZenpЕҚ-kЕҚen-fun|1=ZenpЕҚ-kЕҚen-fun}} |58m |Late 6th century |}
Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun is located on a low terrace at the northwestern foot of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|дҝЎеӨӘеұұдёҳйҷө|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinodayama Hills|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎеӨӘеұұжј”зҝ’е ҙ}} in southern Osaka. The name "Kaibukiyama" derives from the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}} when farmers gathered here during the Chihara Revolt, using conch shells (hЕҚragai) as signals.Historic Site Intypeation Board, Izumi City, 2005. Partial archaeological investigations have been conducted.ЕҢtani, Takeshi. "Kaibukiyama Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨеўіеӨ§иҫһе…ё|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The mound is in the type of a short-tailed {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|еёҶз«ӢиІқеҪўеҸӨеўі|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with the short tail facing northwest. It measures approximately 60 meters in total length, with the rear circular section having a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 7.7 meters. The mound surface reveals {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=Fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}} and {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} fragments (both cylindrical and figurative). Surrounding the mound is a moat. burial goods recovered include haniwa fragments and a steatite trough. The burial chamber's structure remains unknown. The tumulus is estimated to have been built in the early {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}}, during the middle period of the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}."Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|е’ҢжіүгҒ® Municipal History6|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi City History Volume 6|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65вҖ“70. Archaeological studies suggest that the northern and southern burial clusters of Izumi alternated as the primary burial sites for regional chiefs."Report on the Excavation of the ЕҢzono Site," Izumi City Education Board, 2014, pp. 1вҖ“4. In 2003, the mound was Historic Site1 a historic site by Izumi City.{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai201804.pdf Izumi City Designated Historic Site]}} (Izumi City official website). File:Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Full view of the mound
